Timeline / Phases: 6+ weekly phases: wks 1-2, 2-4, 4-6, 6-8, 8-12, 12+; PT 1-2x/wk throughout

Source Organization: Advanced Orthopedics and Sports Medicine (Houston Spine Surgeon)
WB Status: WBAT from POD 1; walking to ½ mile daily or stationary bike 15-30 min

Source Organization: Ohio State University Wexner Medical Center

WB Status: WBAT from POD 1; no formal WB restrictions; walking goal 30 min 2x/day Phase 1

WB Status: WBAT from POD 1; walking 10-15 min 1-2x/day Phase 1; driving when off narcotics and tolerating 15 min sitting

Source Organization: Advanced Health and Sports Medicine Physiotherapy (AHSMP)
WB Status: WBAT; gentle walking <10 min Phase 1; progress to 20 min by wk 4
Key Restrictions: No spinal bending/twisting x 2 wks; no lifting x 2 wks; log roll to get out of bed; avoid prolonged positions; avoid hamstring stretch if sciatic nerve irritation persists; lifting max 10% BW wks 2-4, 15% BW wks 4-6; avoid end-range lumbar ROM
Timeline / Phases: 6+ phases (weekly): wks 1-2, 2-4, 4-6, 6-8, 8-12, 12+; PT 1-2x/wk throughout
Notes: International protocol (Australia); 2023; real-time ultrasound for TA/PF activation wks 1-2; Pilates reformer integrated Phase 2+; hydrotherapy optional; manual therapy adjacent to (not at) operated levels; virtually identical structure to AHSMP discectomy protocol; low-grade manual therapy to lumbar segments adjacent to operated levels; criterion-based progression

Source Organization: Advanced Health and Sports Medicine Physiotherapy (AHSMP)
WB Status: WBAT; gentle walking <10 min Phase 1; progress to 20 min by wk 4
Key Restrictions: No spinal bending/twisting x 2 wks; no lifting x 2 wks; log roll to get out of bed; avoid prolonged positions; avoid hamstring stretch if ongoing sciatic nerve irritation; small ROM spinal movements only wks 2-4; lifting max 10% BW wks 2-4, 15% BW wks 4-6
Timeline / Phases: 6+ phases (weekly): wks 1-2, 2-4, 4-6, 6-8, 8-12, 12+; PT 1-2x/wk throughout
Notes: International protocol (Australia); real-time ultrasound for TA/pelvic floor activation wks 1-2; Pilates reformer integrated from Phase 2; hydrotherapy optional; manual therapy for hip and thoracic mobility; low-grade manual therapy to lumbar segments adjacent to operated levels (avoid op site); criterion-based progression each phase; 2023

Source Organization: Ohio State University Wexner Medical Center
Surgeon(s) / Author(s): Julia Rose EdD PA-C; OSU Neurological Surgery / Orthopaedic Surgery
WB Status: WBAT from POD 1; no formal WB restrictions; walking goal 30 min 2x/day Phase 1
Key Restrictions: No NSAIDs (per surgeon); no driving while on narcotics; no scar mobilization x 3 months; no brace; no BLTs (bending/lifting/twisting) >20 lbs x 3 months; no sitting >30-45 min at a time; no stationary bike or rower; no lumbar hyperextension; no deep hip flexion/extension; avoid submerging incision until healed
Timeline / Phases: 3 phases: Phase 1 (POD 1-3 wks, home focus on ADLs/walking), Phase 2 (3-6 wks, begin OP PT 2-3x/wk x 6-8 wks, 12-24 visits), Phase 3 (6 wks-3 months, progress stabilization and return to baseline)
Notes: .edu site; April 2022; ODI + FABQ at initial eval and 6th visit; walking goal 1 mile in 20 min at 6 wks; pedometer encouraged; lumbar stabilization progression (dead bug, bird dog, bridges, marches); TA and multifidus volitional contraction goals; NO ultrasound; pool therapy optional; return to work timeline varies by job demands; extensive references

WB Status: WBAT from POD 1; walking 1-2x/day starting at 10 min, progressing to 20 min
Key Restrictions: Lifting <15 lbs x 2 wks; no BLTs (bending/twisting) initially; no submerging incision; no lotion on incision; Silverlon dressing removed after 5 days; lumbar spine in neutral — avoid bending/twisting below knees; driving allowed when off narcotics and tolerating 15 min sitting (1-2 wks); notify surgeon if worsening radicular symptoms or progressive weakness
Timeline / Phases: 3 phases: Phase 1 (0-2 wks, wound healing), Phase 2 (2-6 wks, strength training/OP PT), Phase 3 (6-8 wks, advanced strengthening); swimming/elliptical/outdoor bike at 6 wks; running/golf/basketball at 8-12 wks
Notes: Dermabond wound care instructions included; lumbar extension ROM focus (prone press-ups) 0-4 wks; aquatic PT starts at 2 wks; recumbent bike at 2 wks, upright at 4 wks; cupping for scar mobilization; neural mobilization as needed; UE strengthening with TA bracing; includes return-to-sport timelines

WB Status: Per surgeon/operative note; assess inpatient from POD 1; early mobility emphasis
Key Restrictions: Brace if ordered — HOB <30 deg until brace received; log roll for transfers; drain monitoring (Jackson Pratt, hemovac, intrathecal lumbar drains); red flags: new UMN dysfunction (positive Babinski, new clonus, spasticity), fever >101.5, worsening pain; no weight bearing restrictions unless specifically ordered; consult OT for ADL impairments
Timeline / Phases: Inpatient phase covers all spine surgeries (laminectomy, foraminotomy, discectomy, facetectomy, corpectomy, APTIF cervical/thoracic/lumbar); comprehensive Standard of Care format
Notes: BWH inpatient-focused Standard of Care; covers cervical, thoracic, and lumbar; covers all common spine approaches; extensive ICD-10 code list; posture/alignment assessment including kyphosis, scoliosis, orthosis fit; balance training sitting and standing; OT referral for ADL impairments; orthotist referral for custom TLSO/LSCO; extensive references; 2019

WB Status: WBAT from POD 1; walking progression; driving at 2-4 wks when off narcotics and no leg weakness
Key Restrictions: No flexion or extension/rotation beyond neutral; no lifting >15 lbs x 12 wks; no impact activities x 12 wks; no sitting >30 min; brace if >2 level fusion or osteoporotic; bone growth stimulator 6-12 months if prescribed; ALIF: expect ecchymosis/edema into groin (especially men); LLIF: expect anterior thigh numbness and hip flexor/quad weakness on approach side (psoas/lumbosacral plexus irritation, temporary)
Timeline / Phases: 3 phases: Phase 1 (0-2 wks, wound healing/ADLs), Phase 2 (2-6 wks, functional strength training/OP PT), Phase 3 (6-12 wks, advanced progression); return to sport 12+ wks
Notes: Covers all fusion approaches (PSIF/ALIF/LLIF/TLIF) with approach-specific considerations; detailed wound care per incision type (Dermabond vs staples vs nylon sutures); robotic navigation noted; vertebral augmentation (cement) and iliac fixation variants described; fusion with osteotomy; revision PSIF considerations; aquatic PT at 2 wks; Pilates at 6 wks; golf/running/skiing at 12 wks

Source Organization: Ohio State University Wexner Medical Center
Surgeon(s) / Author(s): Julia Rose EdD PA-C; OSU Neurological Surgery / Orthopaedic Surgery
WB Status: WBAT from POD 1; walking goal 30 min 2x/day Phase 1; no formal WB restrictions
Key Restrictions: No NSAIDs x 3-6 months per surgeon; NO BLTs x 12 weeks (no lifting >5-8 lbs x 3 months); no driving while on narcotics; no scar mobilization x 3 months; no tobacco; brace only if poor bone quality, smoker, spinal fracture, or multi-level fusion; outpatient PT typically starts at 12 weeks (vs 3 weeks for discectomy); no sitting >30-45 min; back support with feet flat when sitting
Timeline / Phases: 3 phases: Phase 1 (POD 1-6-12 wks, home walking/ADLs), Phase 2 (typically starts wk 12, OP PT 2-3x/wk x 6-8 wks, 12-24 visits), Phase 3 (return to baseline function/work)
Notes: .edu site; April 2022; key differentiator from discectomy: OP PT starts at 12 wks (not 3 wks); lifting restriction >5-8 lbs (vs >20 lbs for discectomy); same lumbar stabilization program; ODI + FABQ at initial eval and 6th visit; nerve glides 2x10; pool therapy optional; no ultrasound; same authorship as discectomy and laminectomy protocols

WB Status: Mobility per patient tolerance from POD 1; early ambulation encouraged; no strict WB restrictions
Key Restrictions: Caution applying resistance with severe osteoporosis; may not be indicated for recently fractured patients; precaution with spinal ROM per physician orders; monitor for adjacent level fractures; DEXA scan to guide osteoporosis management; fall prevention critical; Genat grading: Grade 1 (20-25% height loss), Grade 2 (25-40%), Grade 3 (>40%)
Timeline / Phases: Inpatient phase + outpatient follow-up; phased progression from pain/edema management to functional strengthening; time based on individual presentation and fracture severity
Notes: BWH Standard of Care; 2011; covers kyphoplasty and vertebroplasty (minimally invasive surgical procedures, NOT conservative management); Genat semiquantitative grading system included; 5-year survival rate for VCF patients 61% vs 76% age-matched peers; primary osteoporosis (postmenopausal) and secondary osteoporosis covered; extension exercises preferred over flexion; paraspinal strengthening + dynamic proprioceptive training; fall prevention education; DEXA scan recommended; outcome tools: VAS, QUALEFFO; note: for non-operative VCF management, see NASS CPG in CPG tab — discrete phased PT protocols for conservative VCF management are uncommon in the literature

WB Status: Sling at least 3 wks (remove 4x/day for exercises/ADLs); sling while sleeping; no driving until off sling and off narcotics
Key Restrictions: No active shoulder motion Phase I; no lifting/carrying with involved arm; no WB through involved arm; no shoulder flexion or ABD >90 deg Phase I; PROM: ER/IR in scapular plane to tolerance + flex/scaption/ABD ≤90 deg; no resistance strengthening Phase II; begin isometrics wks 4-6; begin isotonics wk 6-8 per radiographic healing
Timeline / Phases: 4 phases: Phase I (wks 1-4, PROM ≤90 + ER/IR tolerance; scap retraction/depression; elbow/wrist/hand AROM), Phase II (wks 4-6, AAROM ≤120 then full PROM per tolerance; begin isometrics; sling D/C per surgeon), Phase III (wks 6-12, full ROM goal; isotonic shoulder strengthening per radiographic healing; grade III-IV mobs), Phase IV (wk 12+, advanced strengthening; return to sport/function)
Notes: Criterion-based; bone health, blood supply, pre-op ROM all affect progression; radiographic healing drives isotonic initiation; factors: BMI, smoking, diabetes; complications to monitor: fever, numbness/tingling, drainage

Source Organization: Mammoth Orthopedic Institute / Sierra Park PT
Surgeon(s) / Author(s): Dr. Timothy Crall MD (US Ski Team Physician); Bartlett White PA-C
WB Status: Sling at all times or per MD; no AROM/lifting/pushing/pulling x 6 wks
Key Restrictions: No AROM x 6 wks; no ER >40 deg or excessive extension x 6 wks; no supporting body weight; X-ray evidence of healing required for all phase progressions; no overhead lifting until 4-6 months; PROM FF ≤90 + ER ≤30 Phase I
Timeline / Phases: 4 phases: Phase I (wks 1-3, PROM FF 90 + ER 30; scapular clocks; pendulums; incision mob), Phase II (wks 3-6, AAROM FF 90 + ER 40; pulleys; grade I-II mobs; pool at wk 3), Phase III (wks 6-12, full PROM/AAROM/AROM; submaximal RTC isometrics wk 6; isotonic RTC wk 8; grade III-IV mobs), Phase IV (wk 12+, no overhead until 4-6 months)
Notes: US Ski Team physician; X-ray progression criterion; PT starts wk 1; pool at wk 3; return to work/sport: bilateral AROM + strength 4+/5

WB Status: Sling x 2-4 wks per surgeon; no active shoulder motion x 4 wks; no lifting with operative arm
Key Restrictions: No active shoulder motion x 4 wks; no lifting/carrying with operative arm; no WB through operative arm; sling during sleep Phase I; PROM FF and ABD ≤90 deg Phase I; ROM progression guided by radiographic evidence; no overhead lifting until healing confirmed
Timeline / Phases: 3 phases: Phase I (wks 0-4, sling, PROM ≤90, scapular exercises, elbow/wrist/hand AROM), Phase II (wks 4-8, AAROM progressing to AROM, begin isometrics per radiographic healing), Phase III (wk 8+, progressive strengthening, return to sport/function)
Notes: Evidence-based; 2022; progressive return to overhead per radiographic evidence of healing; covers both mid-shaft and distal clavicle fracture ORIF

Source Organization: Dr. Katherine J. Coyner MD / UConn Musculoskeletal Institute
WB Status: Sling at all times x 6 wks (including sleep); remove for exercises and bathing only
Key Restrictions: No active shoulder motion x 6 wks; no lifting >5 lbs x 6 wks; no horizontal adduction x 6 wks; sling at night x 6 wks; PROM FF ≤90 + ER ≤30 Phase I; no cuff strengthening Phase I-II; no cross-body motion x 6 wks; isometrics at wk 6; isotonics at wk 8; no overhead lifting per MD; incision mobilization after wound healed
Timeline / Phases: 4 phases: Phase I (0-2 wks, sling, pendulums, PROM FF 90 + ER 30), Phase II (wks 2-6, sling, AAROM FF/ER/scaption, grade I-II mobs), Phase III (wks 6-10, D/C sling, AROM, submaximal isometrics wk 6, isotonics wk 8, grade III-IV mobs), Phase IV (wk 10+, progressive strengthening, functional return)
Notes: UConn academic center; sling x 6 wks (most conservative); no horizontal adduction x 6 wks; incision mobilization and desensitization Phase II+; modalities for pain throughout

WB Status: Sling x 6 wks at all times except bathing and ROM exercises; ROM limits: FF 90 + IR/ER 45 Phase I; wk 3 can increase flexion
Key Restrictions: No active ROM Phase I; FF 90 + IR/ER 45 Phase I; no ROM precautions Phase II; no lifting >2 lbs Phase II; stationary bike at wk 3 in sling; no power clean/dead lift/back squats Phase III; fracture healing required for Phase IV
Timeline / Phases: 4 phases: Phase I (0-6 wks, sling, PROM FF 90 + IR/ER 45, scapular stabilization wk 3, deltoid isotonics wk 3, ER/IR isometrics wk 4, stationary bike wk 3), Phase II (7-12 wks, no ROM restrictions, AROM, mobs, Theraband wk 8), Phase III (13-18 wks, progressive strength, walk/jog/run wk 12), Phase IV (19+ wks, return to sport)
Notes: Criterion-based; walk/jog/run progression at wk 12; deltoid and scapular activation early (wk 3); no power lifts Phase III

WB Status: Sling at all times (neutral rotation, 30-45 deg ABD); sleep in sling x 6 wks; shower with arm at side; driving at wk 6 per MD
Key Restrictions: No shoulder AROM Phase I; no lifting/carrying objects x 12 wks; no reaching behind back; no supporting body weight; no bathtub/pool/spa until sutures removed and wound healed; no ABD/ER activity (anterior inferior capsule stress); avoid anterior shoulder stress activities (push-ups, bench press) through Phase III; ER limited to 20 deg Phase I, 45 deg Phase II
Timeline / Phases: 6 phases: Phase I (0-3 wks, PROM begins wk 2: FF <90, ER <20; AAROM wk 3), Phase II (4-5 wks, PROM FF 140, ER 45 scapular plane), Phase III (6-8 wks, AROM, sling wean), Phase IV (9-12 wks, progressive strengthening), Phase V (13-16 wks, plyometrics/sport prep), Phase VI (17+ wks, return to sport)
Notes: Criterion-based; anterior labral/capsular repair specific; sleep in sling x 6 wks; return to sport phase requires full pain-free AROM, 5/5 MMT, no substitution, independent HEP; complications to monitor: fever, numbness/tingling, excessive drainage, uncontrolled pain

Source Organization: Ohio State University Wexner Medical Center
WB Status: Sling per surgeon; ER ≤30 deg x 6 wks
Key Restrictions: ER limited to 30 deg x 6 wks; ROM progression not forced; no jogging until wks 10-12; no Olympic lifting or bar bench press x 6 months; return to sport 5-6 months; contact sports/climbing minimum 6 months; throwing program at month 4 (return to game 9 months); refer back to surgeon with positive apprehension test; Remplissage: no active ER strengthening x 12 wks, no IR or cross-body stretching x 12 wks, no pushing motions, no grade 3-4 posterior mobs x 12 wks; Latarjet (open): subscapularis precautions if taken down — no ER >30 deg, no cross-body ADD, no active IR behind back; no anterior mobs; grade 1 mobs only until wk 6; no cross-body stretching x 12 wks
Timeline / Phases: 4 phases: Phase I Protection (0-6 wks), Phase II Early Loading (6-12 wks), Phase III Intermediate (12-16 wks), Phase IV Return to Sport (4-9 months); throwing program month 4, return to game 9 months
Notes: .edu site; CPG format; covers Bankart, Remplissage, and Latarjet with procedure-specific considerations; risk factors listed (excessive laxity, connective tissue disorders, history of instability); outcome tools: DASH, KJOC; discharge criteria: full AROM, 5/5 MMT shoulder and scapular, no substitution, low pain; Remplissage treated like posterior RTC repair

Surgeon(s) / Author(s): Dr. Theodore Ganley MD; Dr. Kathleen Maguire MD; Dr. Brendan Williams MD; Dr. J. Todd Lawrence MD PhD
WB Status: Sling at all times including sleep except bathing and PT x 6 wks; D/C sling no sooner than wk 6
Key Restrictions: No active FF or ER Phase I (wks 0-6); no passive ER at 90 deg ABD Phase I; no WB/closed chain through involved UE Phase I; ER: ≤30 deg at 20 deg ABD wks 0-3, ≤45 deg wks 3-6; no ROM/stretching beyond staged goals; if hypermobile: avoid aggressive stretching; avoid strengthening with shoulder in end-range horizontal ABD beyond frontal plane (push-ups, bench press, dumbbell flys) Phase II; jogging begins wk 12
Timeline / Phases: 4 phases: Phase I (0-6 wks, PROM/AAROM: FF/ABD ≤90 wks 0-3, ≤135 wks 3-6; scapular stabilization; isometrics), Phase II (6-12 wks, progressive AROM to full by wk 12, WB at wk 10, resistive exercises), Phase III (12-16 wks, jogging, closed chain, UE plyometrics if criteria met, ER/IR at 90 deg), Phase IV (16+ wks, return to sport/full activity)
Notes: 2023 revised; pediatric-focused but applicable to all ages; kinetic chain integration emphasized (lower extremity, trunk, core throughout); power lifting consideration for skeletally mature patients in Phase IV; if criteria met early, patient still must wait for appropriate timeframe; concomitant procedures alter progression — consult surgeon; return to full activity 4-6 months non-throwers

Source Organization: Ohio State University Wexner Medical Center
WB Status: Sling per surgeon; no lifting per surgeon guidance
Key Restrictions: ER in scapular plane: ≤15 deg wks 1-2, ≤30 deg wks 3-4, ≤50 deg wks 5-6, 90 deg wks 7-9; excessive ER above guideline is a precaution; excessive biceps loading is a precaution; no cross-body motion; no shoulder extension with elbow extension; review operative report before initiating rehab — includes reattachment, debridement, or combination; risk factors: overhead activity, FOOSH, contact sports, poor shoulder strength and scapular stability, faulty mechanics
Timeline / Phases: 4 phases: Phase I Weeks 1-2 (PROM: elevation 75 deg scapular plane, ER ≤15 deg; scapular stabilization; submaximal isometrics — no elbow flexion), Weeks 3-4 (PROM: elevation 90 deg, ER ≤30 deg; AAROM begins), Weeks 5-6 (elevation 145 deg, ER ≤50 deg; AROM full can; IR/ER tubing), Phase II Weeks 7-9 (full flex/ABD, ER up to 90 deg), progressive strengthening and return to sport phases
Notes: .edu site; CPG format; SLAP type-specific surgical intervention noted (reattachment vs debridement vs combination — always review op report); risk factors table included; outcome tools: DASH, KJOC; isokinetic strength testing; staged ROM goals very detailed by week; overhead athlete and thrower considerations; discharge criteria: ROM per patient needs, full muscle strength/endurance, MCID on functional outcome

WB Status: Sling x 4 wks while sleeping and walking; D/C at wk 4 unless hyperlax
Key Restrictions: No lifting >coffee cup (8 oz) x 4 wks; no active or resisted IR if open Bankart; ER limited to 25% of opposite shoulder early, progressing to 33% wks 3-4, 50% wks 5-6; no lifting >gallon of milk (8 lbs) wks 5-6; no elbow flexion >90 deg with push-ups (push-up plus only); stationary bike in sling; no IR up the back if open Bankart
Timeline / Phases: Staged weekly progression: POD 1-3 (hand squeezes, elbow/wrist AROM, supported pendulums, scap retraction), POD 4-14 (AAROM FF 90 deg, ER 25% contralateral), Wks 3-4 (FF 120, ER 33%), Wks 5-6 (D/C sling, FF >120, ER 50%), Wks 7-9 (AROM full, RTC tubing/theraband ≤60 deg), Wks 10+ (progressive strengthening, sport-specific)
Notes: .edu site; covers Bankart AND SLAP in same protocol; open Bankart: no active/resisted IR; hyperlax: keep sling beyond wk 4; ER progression as % of contralateral side (25% → 33% → 50% → full); BAPS board on hands; body blade in scaption; pool walking/running noted; 2009 (older but from major academic center)

WB Status: Sling per surgeon, typically 4-6 wks; wean Phase II (wks 5-6); discharge sling Phase III (wks 7-12)
Key Restrictions: No IR Phase I; no horizontal adduction Phase I-III; no UE weight bearing Phase I-II; no overhead activities Phase I-II; no push-ups Phase III; IR behind back to beltline only Phase III; IR in scapular plane limited to 30 deg Phase II, 60 deg Phase III; IR at 90 deg ABD: 30-45 deg by wk 10, progress cautiously to 60-65 deg wk 12; ER to tolerance (unlike anterior where ER is restricted)
Timeline / Phases: 5 phases: Phase I (0-4 wks, PROM: ER to tolerance, FF ≤120, pendulums; isometrics; grade 1-2 traction/inferior glides), Phase II (5-6 wks, AAROM/AROM: FF 140, IR ≤30 scapular plane, ER to tolerance; grade 1-3 mobs), Phase III (7-12 wks, D/C sling, progressive ROM/strength), Phase IV (13-16 wks, advanced strengthening/plyometrics), Phase V (17+ wks, return to sport)
Notes: Key differentiator from anterior: ER to tolerance (not restricted); IR is the restricted motion (opposite of anterior Bankart); no horizontal adduction x 12 wks; isokinetic testing (IR/ER 80-85%) for return to sport; KJOC for overhead athletes; criterion-based; references Dacey et al 2023 OJSM on lack of consensus in posterior stabilization rehab

Source Organization: University of New Mexico Sports Medicine
Surgeon(s) / Author(s): Dr. Gehron Treme MD; Dr. Andrew Veitch MD; Dr. Dustin Richter MD; Dr. Daniel Wascher MD; Dr. Robert Schenck MD
WB Status: Sling/immobilizer at all times wks 0-2; sling during day wks 2-4 (OK to remove at night); D/C sling at 6 wks; PT typically starts at 6 wks
Key Restrictions: No PT wks 0-6 (home program only); keep arm in front of body out of sling — do NOT reach behind back; IR limited to 45 deg (both arm at side and arm at 90 ABD) until 12 wks post-op; no overhead activities until wks 8-10; no overhead presses/military press until month 6; isokinetic strengthening with 60 deg block wks 8-10; IR limited to 45 deg is a key distinguishing precaution
Timeline / Phases: Staged by weeks: 0-2 (immobilizer, distal AROM + pendulums), 2-4 (sling daytime, sub-max isometrics, PROM: FF 60, ext neutral, ABD 90, ER 45, IR neutral only), 6 wks (D/C sling, PROM progresses, Theraband ER, wall walks), 6-8 wks (AROM full except IR ≤45, UBE, wall push-ups, isotonic RTC 0-6 lbs), 8-10 wks (prone strengthening, rhythmic stabilization, isokinetic with 60 deg block), 10-12 wks (RTC to 8-10 lbs), 12+ wks (full IR, functional retraining)
Notes: .edu site (UNM medical school); covers arthroscopic and open posterior labral repair; PT starts 6 wks (later than most protocols — home program only for first 6 wks); IR ≤45 deg until 12 wks is the defining restriction; isokinetic strengthening with 60 deg block at wks 8-10 (speeds 180-60 deg/sec); prone T/Y/W strengthening; no overhead presses x 6 months; 5 faculty surgeons listed

Surgery Type: SLAP Repair, Bankart Repair, or Biceps Tenodesis
Surgeon(s) / Author(s): UVA Orthopaedic Surgery (Brockmeier, Carson, Diduch, Gwathmey, Miller, Werner)
WB Status: Shoulder sling x 6 wks; sleep in sling x 3 wks
Key Restrictions: No resisted active isolated biceps (elbow flexion or forearm supination) x 6 wks; no active ER, extension, or ABD Phase I (wks 0-4); ER ≤30 deg x 4 wks, ER ≤50 deg wks 5-6; no biceps loading until wk 10; no throwing or overhead sports until wk 20; full ROM should be achieved by wks 8-10; ER at 90 deg ABD up to 120 deg for throwers
Timeline / Phases: 3 phases: Phase I Protective (day 1-wk 6 — wks 0-2 PROM FF/scaption 90 + ER ≤30; wks 3-4 advance IR to 60; wks 5-6 FF 145 + ER 50 + AAROM to 90 deg), Phase II Moderate Protection (wks 7-12 — isotonic IR/ER wks 7-9, biceps at wk 10, aggressive exercises Phase III: wks 10-12), Phase III Minimum Protection (wks 13-20 — light plyometrics wks 13-16, sport-specific wks 16-20)
Notes: .edu site; covers SLAP repair, Bankart repair, and biceps tenodesis in one combined protocol; 6 UVA surgeons listed; biceps tenodesis-specific restriction noted (no isolated biceps x 6 wks, no supination resistance); overhead athletes: ER at 90 ABD up to 120 deg; PT 2-3x/wk through wk 12; throwers: no overhead sports until wk 20

Surgery Type: Humeral Fracture ORIF (Proximal, Mid-Shaft, or Distal)
WB Status: Sling full time x 2 wks; outside home only wks 2-6; D/C at wk 6; sleep arm out of sling on elevated pillows for edema
Key Restrictions: No aggressive or forced PROM until X-rays show full union; no IR behind back or horizontal adduction; no lifting >1-2 lbs; no ABD — wall walking and table slides only for flexion/ABD; no anterior or inferior GH mobs wks 6-9; no overhead lifting until 4-6 months; strengthening only if overall pain level is low; covers proximal, mid-shaft, AND distal humerus ORIF
Timeline / Phases: 4 time frames: Wks 1-4 (sling, PROM FF/ABD 90 + ER as tolerated; scap shrugs/retraction; wall walking/table slides; ice/modalities), Wks 4-6 (D/C sling at wk 6; ROM all planes as tolerated; serratus exercise wks 5-6; no-load ER/IR with towel roll; submaximal isometrics), Wks 6-9 (posterior capsule stretches; UBE; scapular strength; Theraband IR/ER/flex/ABD; rows no beyond plane of body; CKC: quadruped → ball compression → wall push-ups; rhythmic stabilization wks 9-12 at 110-120 flex), 3-6 months (advance RTC/scapular/UE strength; no overhead lifting until 4-6 months)
Notes: July 2022; unique: covers proximal AND mid-shaft AND distal humerus ORIF in one protocol; sleep with arm out of sling on elevated pillows for edema control; serratus exercise at wks 5-6; no anterior/inferior GH mobilization wks 6-9; kinesthetic awareness supine exercise in ER/IR wks 6-9; CKC progression (quadruped → ball compression → wall push-ups → perturbations); 10 physicians listed

WB Status: Sling worn only as desired (not mandated for daytime); gentle passive pendulums immediately; no active shoulder motion x 4 wks
Key Restrictions: No active shoulder motion x 4 wks; gentle passive shoulder motion all planes without restrictions from day 1 (no ROM limits stated unlike other protocols); no cuff strengthening until active ROM full (3-6 months); scapulohumeral kinematics normalization emphasized throughout
Timeline / Phases: 4 phases: Phase I 0-6 wks (2x/wk PT + daily home HEP 5x/day; sling worn as desired; PROM no restrictions; AROM neck/elbow/wrist/hand; pendulums 3x/day; icing 3-5x/day), Phase II 6 wks-3 months (2-3x/wk PT; PROM/AAROM all planes; periscapular strength; progress to AROM; isometrics when active ROM adequate), Phase III 3-6 months (1-2x/wk PT; RTC strengthening bands/dumbbells when full AROM achieved), Phase IV 6-9 months (4-5x/wk HEP; high rep/low weight full UE + RTC + periscapular strengthening)
Notes: Covers proximal humerus AND greater tuberosity ORIF; notable: most permissive protocol — no ROM restrictions for PROM, sling worn only as desired; discharge criteria: maximize ROM + full ADLs + normal scapulohumeral rhythm >100 deg elevation + independent HEP; longer overall timeline (9 months); periscapular strength emphasized equally with RTC throughout

Surgery Type: Proximal Humerus Fracture / Greater Tuberosity Avulsion — Non-Operative
WB Status: Sling per MD; monitor per MD instructions; wean per clinical progression
Key Restrictions: Fracture stability is variable — check specifications and precautions from MD; greater tuberosity avulsion included; pendulums begin 7-10 days post fracture; passive shoulder motion from day 7-10; AAROM ER limited to 40 deg until wk 3; isometrics not until wk 4; hyperextension AAROM not until wk 6
Timeline / Phases: Early Passive Motion (days 7-10), Early Passive Motion Phase 2 (days 10-21, AAROM begins, ER ≤40 deg), Phase I (wks 3-6, isometrics at wk 4, AAROM hyperextension at wk 6), Phase II (wks 6-8, active FF supine + standing with stick, pulleys with eccentric lowering), Phase III (wk 8+, resistive exercises + advanced stretching, scapular rotator strengthening)
Notes: Covers both proximal humerus fracture and greater tuberosity avulsion fracture; eccentric lowering with pulleys Phase II; pulleys with eccentric control emphasized; scapular rotators for proper scapulohumeral rhythm; humeral head depression strength emphasized (to avoid impingement); bilateral hanging stretches Phase III; TENS for pain control

WB Status: Sling at least 3 wks (remove 4x/day for exercises and ADLs); wean at wk 4; no driving until adequate ROM, sling D/C, off narcotics
Key Restrictions: No ABD >90 deg Phase I; ER 0-40 deg Phase I; no lifting >1 lb Phase I; no lifting >2 lbs before 6 wks; no forceful end-range overpressure; no isotonic shoulder strengthening Phase II; no motions into painful ranges; bone health, blood supply, pre-op ROM/strength all affect progression — communicate with surgeon
Timeline / Phases: 4 phases: Phase I (wks 1-4, PROM: FF 140, ER 40, ABD 90; pendulums; scap retraction), Phase II (wks 4-8, AAROM then AROM at wk 6; lawn chair/table slides/pulleys; shoulder isometrics; biceps/triceps/prone rows), Phase III (wks 8-12, AROM, progressive strengthening — RTC, deltoid, periscapular), Phase IV (wks 12+, return to function/sport)
Notes: Criterion-based; PROM goals to progress: FF 140 + ER 40 + ABD 90 by wk 4; AROM starts at 6 wks explicitly; biceps tenodesis co-procedure: avoid resistance elbow flexion x 6 wks; factors affecting outcomes noted: bone health, blood supply, BMI, smoking, diabetes; complications to monitor: fever, numbness, drainage, pain

Notes: US Ski Team physician; PT starts wk 1 (earliest of all protocols here); X-ray progression criterion throughout; pool at wk 3; pec minor stretching Phase III for scapular protraction; posterior scapular stretching wk 8 if needed; return to work/sport: bilateral AROM + 4+/5 strength

WB Status: Shoulder immobilizer x 8 wks (worn at all times except ROM exercises; D/C completely at wk 8; worn at night wks 0-6); no active motion x 8 wks; no active ABD x 10 wks

Notes: Single-surgeon protocol (Dr. Bott); 2023; also indicated for exceedingly poor tissue quality regardless of tear size; explicit PROM ROM milestones per week (FF 110 wk 3-4, 130 wk 5-6); progression criteria: FF 130 deg + ER 45 deg at side by wk 6; Phase IV initiation of supraspinatus/infraspinatus strengthening at months 3-4; subscapularis variant explicitly noted in Phase II with reduced ER limit

Surgery Type: Rotator Cuff Repair — Small to Medium Tears (<1–4 cm)
WB Status: Sling 24 hrs/day x 4-6 wks; remove for bathing/dressing/exercises; keep arm relaxed in sling
Key Restrictions: No AROM or resisted ROM Phase I (wks 0-4); no IR or extension initially — all PROM starts in scapular plane; no behind back movements (avoid combined ext/add/IR); no lifting/carrying; no pushing/pulling; subscapularis repair: limit extension to neutral x 6 wks, ER to neutral x 4 wks then >neutral to tolerance until wk 6, no isolated IR resistance x 12 wks; open repair: limit extension/ER to neutral x 6 wks, no active flexion x 6-8 wks, no IR resistance x 6-8 wks (deltoid detachment); Regeneten augmentation: sling 4 wks, AROM at wk 4, strengthening at wk 8; no aggressive strengthening x 12 wks; strength goal 85-90% by 5-6 months
Timeline / Phases: 4 phases: Phase I (0-4 wks, max protected PROM/safe AAROM), Phase II (4-8 wks, AROM begins wk 6, sub-max isometrics), Phase III (8-12 wks, progressive strengthening), Phase IV (12+ wks, advanced/return to sport/work); strength training should continue ≥1 year post-op
Notes: Evidence-based; outlined for double-row suture bridge (trans-osseous) supraspinatus repair; covers arthroscopic and mini-open; includes separate sub-protocols for: subscapularis repair, open repair, and Regeneten augmentation; pre-op stiffness correlated to post-op stiffness (best predictor: pre-op IR behind back vertebral level); blood flow restriction (BFR) exercises noted as optional; create posterior-dominant shoulder (emphasize ER and scapular retractors); no pulleys wks 0-4; small <1 cm, medium 2-4 cm (their definitions); large/massive 5+ cm

Timeline / Phases: 6 phases: Phase I (0-3 wks, PROM only), Phase II (4-6 wks, AAROM, sling at night), Phase III (7-8 wks, AROM, discontinue sling), Phase IV (9-10 wks, light resistance), Phase V (11-14 wks, progressive strengthening), Phase VI (15+ wks, return to function/sport)

Key Restrictions: No shoulder AROM Phase I; no reaching behind back (especially IR); no excessive ER or ABD Phase I-II (ER ≤30 deg Phase I, ER ≤30 deg scapular plane Phase II); no lifting >coffee cup through Phase III; no supporting body weight with hands; avoid shoulder hyperextension (pillow behind elbow in supine x 6-8 wks); rotator cuff repair concomitant: progress by criteria not time; secondary to fracture: same criterion-based approach
Timeline / Phases: 6 phases: Phase I (0-3 wks, PROM), Phase II (4-6 wks, AAROM/AROM + periscapular/RTC), Phase III (7-8 wks, full ROM, discontinue sling), Phase IV (9-10 wks, light resistance), Phase V (11-14 wks, progressive strengthening), Phase VI (15+ wks, return to function)
Notes: Covers TSA and hemiarthroplasty; criterion-based progression; RTC external rotators activated Phase II; motor control (rhythmic stabilization) Phase II; sidelying horizontal ADD stretch Phase II; covers rotator cuff repair and fracture arthroplasty variants; complications to monitor: fever, numbness/tingling, excessive drainage, uncontrolled pain, acromion tenderness

Surgeon(s) / Author(s): Reg B. Wilcox III PT; Dept. of Rehabilitation Services
WB Status: Sling continuously 3-4 wks; no shoulder AROM Phase I; no driving x 3 wks
Key Restrictions: No AROM Phase I; no motion behind back especially IR; no excessive ER or sudden movements; no supporting body weight; no soaking incision x 2 wks; pillow behind elbow in supine x 6-8 wks ('always visualize elbow while on back'); no heavy lifting >coffee cup through Phase II; if concomitant RC repair or fracture/cuff arthropathy: progress by criteria not time; forceful stretching not indicated if ROM criteria not met — use Grade I oscillations; PROM ≠ stretching (emphasized)
Timeline / Phases: 3+ phases: Phase I Immediate Post-Surgical (wks 0-4, PROM progressive), Phase II (wks 4-8, AAROM/AROM, initiate RC), Phase III (wks 8-12+, progressive strengthening, return to function); treatment algorithm decision tree included
Notes: 2016; 'PROM is not stretching!!!!!!!!' explicitly noted; SPADI at first ambulatory visit, every 30 days and discharge; Phase I criteria: ≥90 deg PROM FF, ≥45 deg ER scapular plane, ≥70 deg IR scapular plane at 30 deg ABD; references Wilcox et al 2005 JOSPT as foundational protocol; treatment algorithm (decision tree) for progressing the rehab program

WB Status: WBAT with crutches wk 1 (balance only); encourage FWB ASAP; advance per tolerance
Key Restrictions: Knee immobilizer 48 hrs only (exception: concomitant MCL tear); avoid all open chain x 12 wks; open chain max 25 lbs wks 13-16; BTB bridge program begins wk 12; functional brace for contact sports only
Timeline / Phases: Week 1 (limited activity, WBAT, SLR 50 2x/day); Wks 2-6 (ROM: 0-120 wk 4, 0-130+ wk 6; CKC when no limp); Wks 7-8 (aggressive ROM, aerobic); Wks 9-12 (jogging/swimming/golf with MD OK at wk 8, plyometrics); Wks 13-16 (OC up to 25 lbs, retro training); Wks 17-24 (return to sport)
Notes: Surgeon protocol; patellar glides 4x/day wks 1-6; return to sport: painless ROM = contralateral, no effusion, quad control, excellent agility/proprioception; Get Set Train Smarter app recommended; functional brace evidence-based for contact football/soccer/rugby

WB Status: WBAT with crutches wk 1 (balance only); encourage FWB ASAP; advance per tolerance
Key Restrictions: Knee immobilizer 48 hrs only (exception: concomitant MCL tear); avoid all open chain x 12 wks; open chain max 25 lbs wks 17-24; hamstring bridge program begins wk 16; functional brace for contact sports only
Timeline / Phases: Week 1 (limited activity, WBAT, SLR 50 2x/day); Wks 2-8 (ROM: 0-120 wk 4, 0-130+ wk 6; CKC when no limp); Wks 9-12 (aggressive ROM, aerobic); Wks 13-16 (jogging/swimming/golf with MD OK at wk 12, plyometrics); Wks 17-24 (OC up to 25 lbs, retro training); Wk 25+ (return to sport)
Notes: Hamstring graft variant: jogging begins wk 12 (vs wk 8 BTB), bridge program delayed to wk 16, OC begins wk 17; return to sport: painless ROM = contralateral, no effusion, quad control, excellent agility/proprioception; Get Set Train Smarter app recommended

WB Status: PWB 40-60 lbs with crutches wks 0-2; PWB 80-100 lbs wks 3-4; D/C crutches after wk 4
Key Restrictions: Knee immobilizer full time wks 0-2; NO SLR wks 0-2; CKC before OC quad strengthening; McConnell taping if patellofemoral pain present wks 9-12; return to sport requires MD clearance
Timeline / Phases: Wks 0-2 (PWB 40-60 lbs, immobilizer, quad sets/ankle pumps, well leg bike/UE ergometer); Wks 3-4 (D/C immobilizer, AROM, crutches PWB 80-100 lbs, foot/ankle mobility); Wks 5-8 (formal PT, CKC quads, SLR, patellar mobs, OC hams, D/C crutches wk 4, hip mobility/LE control); Wks 9-12 (OC quads/hams, McConnell taping if needed); Wks 13-24 (HEP 3x/wk, return to sport with MD ok)
Notes: Fulkerson/Elmslie-Trillat osteotomy for patellar maltracking; hip mobility and LE control emphasized mid-phase; patellar mobilization central post-op; no specific RTS functional criteria listed - MD clearance required

WB Status: PWB 40-60 lbs with crutches wks 0-4; WBAT unprotected when no limp and good quad control (wks 4-6+)
Key Restrictions: Knee immobilizer full time wks 0-2; no resistance exercises wks 4-6; no kneeling or squatting until after wk 8; does NOT apply to ACL + meniscal repair combined (follow ACL protocol); return to sport with MD ok after minimum 4 months
Timeline / Phases: Wks 0-4 (PWB 40-60 lbs, immobilizer wks 0-2 then D/C, AROM as tolerated, quad sets/SLR/ankle pumps, well leg bike); Wks 4-6 (WBAT when no limp/quad control, AROM stretches, goal full ROM by wk 6, stationary bike no resistance); Wks 6-8 (initiate CKC strengthening, no kneeling/squatting); Wks 8-12 (initiate open chain strengthening); Wks 12-16 (protected agility and sports-specific); return to sport after 4 months with MD ok
Notes: Explicitly for isolated meniscal repair only; ACL + meniscal repair patients follow ACL protocol; RTS minimum 4 months with MD clearance; no specific functional RTS criteria listed

WB Status: WBAT with crutches wk 1 (balance only); encourage FWB ASAP
Key Restrictions: Knee brace locked in full extension x 2 wks; transition out of brace wks 3-4; D/C brace by wk 4; avoid all open chain x 12 wks; open chain limited to 5 lbs initially at wks 13-16
Timeline / Phases: Wk 1 (WBAT, brace locked extension, SLR 50 2x/day, ankle pumps, ted hose); Wks 2-6 (ROM: full extension by POD 7, 0-130 by wk 4; CKC when no limp, no OC x 12 wks, well leg/UE conditioning); Wks 7-8 (aggressive ROM, aerobic); Wks 9-12 (jogging/golf/swimming with MD OK at wk 8); Wks 13-16 (OC 5 lbs max if full painless ROM); return to sport with MD clearance
Notes: Similar framework to ACL protocol; brace locked 0 deg x 2 wks then weaned; open chain begins wk 13 at 5 lb max; return to sport: full painless ROM, no effusion, quad control, excellent agility/proprioception

WB Status: PWB with crutches x 6 wks; progressive WB wks 6-12; FWB Phase III+
Key Restrictions: Brace locked 0/0 deg x 6-8 wks; ROM limited 0-90 deg passive prone flexion or seated (anterior tibial translation technique) x 6 wks; no resisted hamstrings x 12 wks; minimize posterior tibial translation throughout; quad sets with towel behind tibia
Timeline / Phases: Phase I Max Protection (wks 0-6): PWB, brace locked, patellar mobs, ROM 0-90, quad sets/SLR; Phase II Progressive (wks 6-12): brace open 0-90, progress ROM, gym strengthening (leg press/calf/abd-add - NO hamstrings), bilateral CKC squats, stationary bike, pool; Phase III Advanced (wks 12-20): full ROM, advance cardio, begin resisted hamstrings; Phase IV Return to Sport (wks 20 to 9-12 mo): pool running wk 20, dry land running wk 24, agility drills 7-8 mo, return to sport 9-12 mo
Notes: Quadriceps-dominant protocol; posterior tibial shear avoided throughout; resisted hamstrings held until wk 12; pool running precedes dry land; return to sport 9-12 months; anterior tibial translation technique during early ROM protects graft

WB Status: NWB with crutches x 6 wks; progressive WB wks 6-12; FWB Phase III+
Key Restrictions: Brace locked 0 deg x 6 wks; ROM 30-90 deg WITH VALGUS STRESS APPLIED wks 0-4; NO hyperextension at any phase; limit flexion to 90 deg until wk 4; no varus stress; functional brace fitting at wk 20+; sports test required for return to competition
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 Max Protection (wks 0-6): NWB, brace locked 0, patellar mobs, ROM 30-90 with valgus stress, quad sets/SLR in brace; Phase 2 Progressive (wks 6-12): progressive WB, bilateral CKC squats, hip strengthening, step-up, gym strengthening, stationary bike/walking/elliptical; Phase 3 Advanced (wks 12-20): full ROM, advance cardio, CKC bilateral to unilateral, pool running wk 16; Phase 4 Plyometric (wks 20-24): straight plane running progressing to lateral/rotational, plyometrics bilateral to unilateral, functional brace fitting, sports test
Notes: NWB x 6 wks (stricter than PCL); valgus stress during early ROM protects PLC repair from varus loading; no hyperextension throughout all phases; sports test required before competition clearance; one of the longer more conservative knee protocols

WB Status: WBAT with walker/crutches from DOS; advance to crutches when able; unprotected WB when no limp (wk 6+); D/C to cane if limp at wk 6
Key Restrictions: CPM 0-120 started DOS; ROM goals mandatory - notify MD immediately if not met (0-100 by DC, 0-110 wk 2, 0-120 wk 4); max expected ROM 0-135; extension emphasis equivalent to ACL rehab
Timeline / Phases: DOS (OOB to chair, WBAT walker/crutches, CPM 0-120, ankle pumps, quad sets, stair instructions, OT eval for home aids); POD 1-3 (OOB 2x/day, advance to crutches if able, CPM, ROM exercises, stair training); POD 6-30 (CKC, ROM: 0-110 wk 2, 0-120 wk 4); Wks 1-6 (crutch/cane per MD, stationary bike ASAP goal 15-20 min 3x/wk, aggressive CKC); Wks 6-24 (unprotected WB if no limp, gait training, stationary bike 20-30 min/day)
Notes: Comprehensive joint program; spinal anesthetic + femoral and sciatic nerve blocks; CPM started DOS; most patients DC POD 2; extension emphasized as aggressively as ACL; OT eval for home aids (reachers, toilet risers); stationary bike initiated ASAP

WB Status: PWB 40-60 lbs with crutches wk 1; WBAT / D/C crutches wk 2
Key Restrictions: Knee wrap on - do not remove unless instructed by MD; if effusion develops with activity reduce intensity and modify program; advance to plyometrics/running only if knee quiet with no effusion and patient pain-free
Timeline / Phases: Wk 1 (PWB 40-60 lbs, quad sets/SLR); Wk 2 (WBAT, D/C crutches, AROM flexion/extension if minimal effusion, patellar mobs/lateral retinacular stretches, edema control, foot/ankle mobility review); Wks 3-6 (progressive active extension, CKC strengthening and aerobic program if minimal effusion, hip abd/extension strengthening); Wks 6-12 (plyometrics/sports-specific agility/running if knee quiet, then dismiss to HEP)
Notes: Effusion is primary limiter of progression throughout; patellar mobilization and lateral retinacular stretching central to recovery; relatively quick recovery vs ligament reconstructions; dismiss to HEP at wk 12

WB Status: NWB x 6 wks; WB 25% per week in boot starting wk 7; unrestricted WB after wk 12 if radiographic union
Key Restrictions: Complete immobilization in boot/cast wks 1-6; boot with orthotic wks 7-12; ASO lace-up brace with medial strap and orthotic wks 12-24; orthotic in all shoes long-term; increased swelling/pain may indicate incomplete bone healing - reduce WB and therapy demands accordingly
Timeline / Phases: Pre-op training 1 wk before surgery; Phase I Immobilization (wks 0-6, NWB); PT begins after 6 wks immobilization; Phase II Early Rehab (wks 7-12, boot with orthotic, 25% WB/wk, AROM only first 2 wks then passive if needed, isometrics then progress CKC to OC); Phase III Brace/Return (wks 12-24, ASO brace, unrestricted WB if radiographic union, proprioception progression)
Notes: Reconstruction often includes osteotomies with bone healing requirements; PT delayed until 6 wks post-op; must achieve dorsiflexion and inversion ROM; strength goal: PF and inversion with single-leg stance; proprioception goal: single-leg balance eyes closed 20 seconds; orthotic required long-term in all footwear

Surgery Type: Arthroscopic and Open Rotator Cuff Repair
WB Status: N/A; sling and swathe full time wks 1-6; sling daytime / swathe for sleep wks 5-6; discontinued end of wk 6
Key Restrictions: No active shoulder motion until wk 5-6; no lifting with operative hand until wk 6-8; avoid full cuff stress 4 months; no ballistic activities or push/pull exercises long term
Timeline / Phases: Wks 1-2 (sling, hand/wrist/elbow ROM only); Wks 3-4 (PT begins, passive ROM); Wks 5-6 (AAROM, sling weaned); Wks 6-8 (AROM, eccentrics arm weight only); Wks 8-12 (progressive resistance up to 5 lbs); Wks 13-16 (HEP, up to 10 lbs); Wks 17-20 (up to 20 lbs); Wks 21-24 (up to 40 lbs); Wk 24+ (return to throwing/racket sports)
Notes: Throwing sports progression at 6-9 months; push-ups/pull-ups/bench press generally not advised long term for older patients due to recurrent tear risk

WB Status: N/A; sling and swathe full time day and night x 4 wks
Key Restrictions: Avoid ABD/ER combination x 8 wks to protect anterior inferior glenohumeral ligament complex; no bench press/flies/military press in ABD/ER position through wk 12-16
Timeline / Phases: Wks 0-4 (sling/swathe, isometrics); Wks 5-6 (AROM initiated); Wks 7-8 (progressive ROM, PREs begin); Wks 9-11 (up to 5 lbs); Wks 12-16 (no sports, up to 10 lbs); Month 4-6 (sport-specific progressions); Month 6+ (return to sports with surgeon approval)
Notes: Throwing sports: graduated toss program at 4 months; contact sports no earlier than 6 months; return to sport individualized per strength/ROM/repair security

WB Status: N/A; sling and swathe full time day and night x 4 wks
Key Restrictions: No IR past anterior axillary line (posterior repair precaution); avoid bench press/flies/military press/posterior capsular stretching x 6 months; no sports until 9 months post-op
Timeline / Phases: Wks 0-4 (sling/swathe, immobilization); Wks 5-6 (initiate AROM); Wks 6-8 (stretching/joint mob); Wks 9-12 (PRE); Wks 12-16 (advance PRE, no sports); Wks 17-24 (return to light activity)
Notes: Full ROM goal at 12 wks; no water skiing 1 year; unrestricted throwing and competitive swimming at 1 year; graded throwing program begins at 9 months

WB Status: N/A; lifting restricted to 5 lbs operative arm wks 0-4; 10 lbs max wks 5-6
Key Restrictions: Limit lifting to 5 lbs all planes wks 0-4; advance to 10 lbs wks 5-6; RTC eccentrics kept below 90 degrees early
Timeline / Phases: Wks 0-4 (AAROM to AROM, scapular control, RTC eccentrics); Wks 5-6 (PRE, functional patterns); Wks 7-12 (progressive strengthening, plyometrics, full recovery goal); Wks 12-24 (home maintenance)
Notes: Condensed protocol with no formal phase names or sling/immobilization instructions; goal is full recovery by 12 wks post-op

Surgery Type: Distal Bicep Tendon Repair — Single Incision Endo Button
WB Status: N/A; elbow hinge brace blocking extension ~20 degrees wks 1-6
Key Restrictions: Elbow hinge brace blocking extension at ~20 degrees through wk 6; weight limit 5 lbs at wk 6; no progressive strengthening until wks 9-10
Timeline / Phases: Wks 1-2 (edema control, AROM in brace); Wk 3 (scar management, brace continued); Wk 6 (brace D/C, passive extension begun); Wks 9-10 (progressive strengthening); Wk 12 (full use)
Notes: Listed under Shoulder and Arm section on Mountain Ortho website; single-incision endo button technique

WB Status: N/A; sling/immobilizer full time wks 1-4; discontinued at wk 4-5 unless MD instructs otherwise
Key Restrictions: No biceps resistance x 6 wks; avoid full stress to superior labrum and biceps x 4 months; no jogging/bouncing until 12 wks post-op
Timeline / Phases: Wks 1-4 (PROM, sling full time); Wks 5-6 (AAROM/AROM transition, sling D/C); Wk 7 (gentle biceps resistance); Wks 8-12 (progressive resistance up to 5 lbs); Wks 12-16 (up to 10 lbs, jogging begins); Wks 17-24 (return to sport with surgeon clearance)
Notes: Throwing: short toss at 4 months, full throwing at 6 months; racket sports baseline at 4 months, serving/overheads at 6 months

WB Status: N/A; sling and swathe full time initially; sling daytime only wks 2-4; discontinued with MD approval wks 4-6
Key Restrictions: No active IR until wk 6; ER within surgeon-defined limits through wk 4; elevation capped at 120 degrees until wk 6
Timeline / Phases: 7 phases: POD 1-2 (hospital); POD 3-10; Wks 2-4; Wks 4-6; Wks 6-8; Wks 8-12; Wks 12-24 (home program); progresses from passive ROM to full strengthening/conditioning by 6 months
Notes: Codman pendulums initiated early through wk 4; PREs introduced wks 6-8; elevation goal 140-160 degrees by wks 8-12

WB Status: Initially brace locked with crutches; WBAT when pain-free; allograft/HS autograft PWB x6 weeks
Key Restrictions: No activities causing locking or giving way; no pivoting
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-2), Phase II (Wks 3-5), Phase III (Wks 6-8), Phase IV (8-12+ wks return to sport)

WB Status: FWB throughout
Key Restrictions: Avoid: descending stairs, prolonged sitting, running, jumping
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-2 acute), Phase II (Wks 2-4 sub-acute), Phase III (Wks 4-8 remodeling)

WB Status: PWB wks 0-1; WBAT with crutches; brace locked until quad control achieved; FWB ~wks 3-6
Key Restrictions: Flexion limited initially; no pivoting; brace locked for ambulation
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-2), Phase II (Wks 3-6), Phase III (Wks 7-12), Phase IV (Wks 13-16 transitional), Phase V (Months 3-5 early RTS), Phase VI (Months 6+ unrestricted)

WB Status: FC lesions: TTWB (20-30%) x6-8 wks; PTG lesions: PWB (50%) x2 wks then WBAT; FWB Phase II
Key Restrictions: No impact exercises; avoid pivoting; no stair-master; no open chain knee extension for PTG lesions
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-8 protection), Phase II (Wks 8-12 progressive loading), Phase III (Wks 12-16 remodeling), Phase IV (Wks 16+ maturation)
Notes: Return to sport 6-12 months depending on lesion size/location

WB Status: NWB x2 weeks all lesions; partial WB per lesion location/size; FWB by 6-10 weeks
Key Restrictions: Hinged brace locked in extension; CPM immediately post-op; no impact until MD cleared
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-6), Phase II (Wks 6-12), Phase III (Months 3-5), Phase IV (Months 5-6 transitional), Phase V (Months 6+ early RTS), Phase VI (Months 8-12 unrestricted)
Notes: Protocol distinguishes between condylar and patellofemoral lesions with different WB progressions

WB Status: FWB throughout
Key Restrictions: Avoid loading knee in flexion; minimize: descending stairs, prolonged sitting, running, jumping
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-2 acute), Phase II (Wks 2-4 sub-acute), Phase III (Wks 4-6 late/chronic)

WB Status: No osteotomy: 25% WB → 50% → 75% → WBAT by wks 5-6 in unlocked brace; with osteotomy: TDWB in locked brace
Key Restrictions: PROM only wks 0-2 (0-40°); no active knee extension 40-70°; no repetitive closed chain knee flexion 40-70°; brace locked at 0° for ambulation
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-6), Phase II (Wks 6-12), Phase III (Wks 12-24), Phase IV (24+ wks advanced strengthening), Phase V (9-12 months early RTS), Phase VI (12 months unrestricted)
Notes: 4 stages of cartilage maturation; size/location of defect guides progression

WB Status: NWB/TDWB brace locked x4 wks; TDWB progressing to PWB wks 4-6; WBAT per MD based on X-ray
Key Restrictions: No active knee extension; no resisted exercise until 6 wks; no running/jumping/plyometrics until 4-6 months
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-6), Phase II (Wks 7-12 protection), Phase III (Wks 13-16 advanced strengthening), Phase IV (16+ wks early RTS)
Notes: Return to sport 7-9 months

WB Status: TDWB (20-30%) in locked brace x4 wks; WBAT with crutches in unlocked brace wks 5-6; discharge brace at 6 wks if SLR without lag
Key Restrictions: No closed chain knee flexion; no open chain extension; brace locked at 0° for WB x2 wks; CPM 6-8 hrs/day
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-6), Phase II (Wks 6-12), Phase III (Wks 12-24), Phase IV (Months 6-9), Phase V (Months 9-12 early RTS), Phase VI (Months 12-18 unrestricted)
Notes: Protocol varies by graft location: anterior vs posterior femoral condyle

WB Status: Initially brace locked with crutches; WBAT when pain/effusion-free with proper gait; allograft/HS autograft PWB x6 weeks
Key Restrictions: No pivot on surgical side; brace until 6 wks per MD; allograft/HS autograft conservative WB x6 wks; more conservative if concomitant meniscus repair
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-2), Phase II (Wks 3-5), Phase III (Wks 6-8), Phase IV (Wks 9-12 transitional), Phase V (Months 3-5 early RTS), Phase VI (Months 6+ unrestricted)
Notes: Different timelines for allograft vs hamstring autograft graft types

WB Status: Brace locked PWB with crutches wks 0-6; D/C brace/crutches at 6 wks per MD once adequate quad control and normalized gait
Key Restrictions: Flexion <90° wks 0-6; avoid active knee flexion (hamstring strain risk on posteromedial joint); no pivoting
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-3), Phase II (Wks 3-6), Phase III (Wks 6-8), Phase IV (Wks 9-12 transitional), Phase V (Months 3-5 early RTS), Phase VI (Months 6+ unrestricted)
Notes: Does not apply to meniscus root repairs or transplants; more conservative for complex tears/all-inside repairs

WB Status: Sling most of time wks 0-2; sleep in sling; wean wks 7-12
Key Restrictions: No active shoulder ROM; no passive ROM >90 deg; no reaching behind/across body; no lifting wks 0-6; no lifting >1lb wks 7-12; no lifting >2-3lbs wks 13-18; no overhead weighted lifting
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-6), Phase II (Wks 7-12), Phase III (Wks 13-18), Phase IV (Wks 19+ advanced strengthening)

WB Status: Sling at all times (neutral rotation, 30-45 deg ABD) wks 0-5; sleep in sling 6 wks; D/C sling wks 6-8
Key Restrictions: No AROM wks 0-5; no carrying objects until 12 wks; avoid ABD/ER; no lifting >10lbs wks 6-11; driving per MD at wk 6; no throwing/overhead until 4 months; avoid wide grip bench press, military press, lat pulldowns behind head
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-3 immediate), Phase II (Wks 4-5 protection), Phase III (Wks 6-8 intermediate), Phase IV (Wks 9-11 transitional), Phase V (Wks 12-16 strengthening), Phase VI (Months 4-6 unrestricted RTS)

WB Status: Sling per surgeon; wean starting wk 3
Key Restrictions: No AROM elbow or shoulder wks 1-4; no shoulder ER >40 deg; no shoulder extension past neutral; no lifting; no loading biceps/elbow flexors/supinators wks 4-6; avoid long-lever arm resistance for elbow flexion/supination wks 6-8; no swimming until wks 8+
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 1-4 PROM), Phase II (Wks 4-6 AROM), Phase III (Wks 6-8 initial strengthening), Phase IV (Wks 8-12 advanced strengthening), Phase V (Wks 12-16 RTS)

WB Status: Sling at night only; D/C wk 6 or per MD
Key Restrictions: No PROM ER past neutral until wk 5; no shoulder AROM until wk 5; no lifting objects; no supporting body weight; avoid excessive ER ROM stretching; avoid load on anterior capsule/subscapularis; limit overstressing anterior capsule with overhead strengthening
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-6 immediate), Phase II (Wks 7-8 intermediate), Phase III (Wks 9-12 late), Phase IV (Wks 13-20 strengthening), Phase V (Wks 21-28 early RTS)
Notes: Procedure for >20% glenoid bone loss; primary functional limitation is reduced glenohumeral ER; ER should be progressed gradually

WB Status: Sling in neutral/IR; sleep in sling; bone-tendon wean at wk 4; tendon-tendon/muscle-tendon wean wks 5-6; WB through surgical side at wks 6-8
Key Restrictions: Very limited PROM initially (ER at 0 deg ADD, flex 45 deg, ABD 30 deg); no shoulder AROM until wks 6-8; no IR/pec major loading until wks 9-14; progressive PROM increases 5-10 deg per week
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-3), Phase II (Wks 4-6), Phase III (Wks 6-8 late), Phase IV (Wks 9-14 transitional), Phase V (Wks 14-20 advanced strengthening), Phase VI (Months 5-6 early RTS), Phase VII (Months 6+ unrestricted RTS)
Notes: Repair type (bone-tendon vs tendon-tendon vs muscle-tendon) dictates sling weaning timeline; 50% 1RM bench press at 6+ months with physician coordination

WB Status: Sling x3 weeks (off 4x/day); sleep in sling until 6 wks; D/C sling at 6 wks; no WB through arm until wks 6-8
Key Restrictions: No active motion involved shoulder; no lifting/carrying; no WB; no flex/ABD >90 deg wks 1-4; no lifting >coffee cup wks 5-8; no lifting >5lbs wks 9-12
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 1-4 immediate), Phase II (Wks 5-8 intermediate), Phase III (Wks 9-12 late), Phase IV (Wks 12-16 transitional), Phase V (Months 4-6 RTS)

WB Status: Sling in neutral rotation with abduction pillow (30-45 deg); sleep in sling; wean during day wks 4-6; D/C at wks 7-8
Key Restrictions: No AROM/AAROM wks 2-3; no PROM into IR wks 2-3; no reaching behind back (especially into IR) throughout; no lifting >coffee cup wks 4-8; no WB through hands; avoid shoulder hyperextension; no lifting >10lbs wks 9-11; no lifting >15lbs wks 12-16
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 2-3 immediate), Phase II (Wks 4-6 intermediate), Phase III (Wks 7-8), Phase IV (Wks 9-11 transitional), Phase V (Wks 12-16 advanced strengthening)
Notes: Deltoid-driven rehab (not rotator cuff); distinct from TSA protocol; outcomes depend on surgical approach and whether RTC repair was performed

WB Status: Sling day and night with abduction pillow (30-45 deg) wks 1-6; gradually removed wks 6-10 with surgeon clearance
Key Restrictions: No AROM or PROM shoulder wks 1-6; no WB; no reaching overhead or behind back; no pushing/pulling; no AROM until wks 10+; no IR until wks 6-10; no lifting >5lbs wks 18-22; no lifting >10lbs wks 22-26; no empty can position at any stage; no straight arm lateral raise ever
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 1-6 immobilization), Phase II (Wks 6-10 PROM), Phase III (Wks 10-18 AAROM/AROM + isometrics wks 14-18), Phase IV (Wks 18-22 initial strengthening), Phase V (Wks 22-26 advanced strengthening), Phase VI (Wks 26-30 RTS)
Notes: Very conservative; submaximal isometrics only wks 14-18; maximal strength testing not until 10-12 months post-op

WB Status: FWB throughout; sling for support if recommended
Key Restrictions: Avoid heavy lifting, overhead activities, and pain-causing activities; progressive return to function
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-4 protection/pain management), Phase II (Wks 4-8 restoring ROM), Phase III (Wks 8-12 progressive strengthening), Phase IV (Wks 12+ return to function)
Notes: For patients not surgical candidates; deltoid-dominant compensatory approach; multidisciplinary team recommended

WB Status: Sling x3 weeks (off 4x/day); wean at wk 4
Key Restrictions: No ABD >90 deg wks 1-4; shoulder ER 0-40 deg wks 1-4; no lifting >1lb wks 1-4; no lifting >2lbs until wk 6; no AROM until wk 6; no isotonic shoulder strengthening wks 4-8; no lifting >10lbs wks 8-12
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 1-4 initial ROM), Phase II (Wks 4-8 AAROM/AROM), Phase III (Wks 8-12 initial strengthening), Phase IV (Wks 12+ advanced strengthening)

WB Status: Sling day and night with abduction pillow (30-45 deg) wks 1-6; gradually removed wks 6-10 with surgeon clearance
Key Restrictions: Same restrictions as small-medium RCR; subscapularis repair: no ER >0 deg, no ABD >90 deg in Phase I; more cautious progression for multi-tendon tears; no empty can position ever; no straight arm lateral raise
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 1-6 immobilization), Phase II (Wks 6-10 PROM), Phase III (Wks 10-18 AAROM/AROM + isometrics wks 14-18), Phase IV (Wks 18-22 initial strengthening), Phase V (Wks 22-26 advanced strengthening), Phase VI (Wks 26-30 RTS)
Notes: Identical timeline to small-medium RCR but more conservative progression; concomitant subscapularis repair requires extra ROM caution in Phase I

WB Status: Sling in neutral with abduction pillow (30-45 deg ABD); sleep in sling; wean during day wks 4-6; D/C at wks 7-8
Key Restrictions: No AROM wks 0-3; no reaching behind back (especially into IR); no excessive ER or ABD; no lifting >coffee cup wks 4-8; no lifting >10lbs wks 7-11; avoid ER above 80 deg ABD; no WB through hands
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-3 immediate), Phase II (Wks 4-6 intermediate), Phase III (Wks 7-8), Phase IV (Wks 9-11 transitional), Phase V (Wks 12-16 advanced strengthening)
Notes: Covers both TSA and hemiarthroplasty; factors include surgical approach, concurrent RTC repair, and reason for arthroplasty

WB Status: Sling in neutral with abduction pillow (30-45 deg); sleep in sling; D/C at wks 7-8
Key Restrictions: No AROM/AAROM wks 0-6; no elbow AROM (avoid biceps contraction) wks 0-8; no lifting wks 0-8; no reaching behind back; no resisted elbow flexion wks 7-12; no lifting >10lbs wks 7-12
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-3 immediate), Phase II (Wks 4-6 intermediate), Phase III (Wks 7-8), Phase IV (Wks 9-12 transitional), Phase V (Wks 13-16 late), Phase VI (Months 4-6 early RTS with throwing program)
Notes: Biceps contraction must be avoided until wk 8; ER at 90 deg ABD should not exceed 90 deg

WB Status: Sling x6 weeks; no WB through involved extremity; no lifting wks 0-6; no lifting >5lbs until wk 9; no lifting restrictions at ~4 months
Key Restrictions: No PROM GH joint wks 0-6; no scapular AROM (protraction/retraction/depression/elevation) wks 0-6; no running/jumping wks 0-6; no lifting >5lbs until wk 9; no repetitive overhead activities wks 6-12
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-6 early post-op), Phase II (Wks 6-12 intermediate), Phase III (Wks 13+ late post-op/return to sport)
Notes: Tendon autograft repair; scapular AROM restricted wks 0-6 to protect reconstruction site

WB Status: Sling day and night x4 weeks; discontinue sling wks 4-8
Key Restrictions: Shoulder ER limited to 30 deg wks 0-4; shoulder IR limited to belt line wks 0-4; shoulder ABD limited to 90 deg wks 0-4; no AROM wks 0-4; no lifting/WB wks 0-4; no shoulder ER at 90 deg ABD wks 4-8; avoid lifting/resisted exercises wks 4-8; no forceful ER stretching wks 8+
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-4 PROM/protection), Phase II (Wks 4-8 AAROM/AROM), Phase III (Wks 8-12 initial strengthening), Phase IV (Wks 12-16 progressive strengthening), Phase V (Wks 16-20 advanced strengthening)

WB Status: FWB throughout; avoid activities that increase intra-abdominal pressure
Key Restrictions: Avoid lifting or activities increasing intra-abdominal pressure throughout; no running until pain-free jogging; hip (flexion/abduction/adduction) LSI <20% before return to running
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Acute - pain control/ROM/isometrics), Phase II (Subacute - PRE/return to running), Phase III (Early RTS - plyometrics/sprinting/agility), Phase IV (Unrestricted RTS)
Notes: Criterion-based; covers adductor/pubic/inguinal/iliopsoas-related groin pain per DOHA 2015 terminology; HAGOS questionnaire >90% for RTS

WB Status: WBAT throughout
Key Restrictions: Limit hamstring stretching (trunk flexion, knee extension) wks 0-2; limit hip/knee ROM to avoid stretch/strain sensation; no jogging until wk 6 if full symmetrical ROM and strength; no sprinting until Phase V (wks 12+)
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-2 early), Phase II (Wks 2-4 intermediate), Phase III (Wks 4-8 transitional), Phase IV (Wks 8-12 early RTS), Phase V (Wks 12+ unrestricted RTS)
Notes: Criterion-based with time guidance; varies by severity, strain grade, and location (proximal/mid-belly/distal); proximal injuries may have concurrent sciatic nerve tension; LSI hamstring/glute/quad >95% for RTP

WB Status: FWB; walking from day 1 (15 min 2x/day easy pace)
Key Restrictions: Avoid lifting or activities that increase intra-abdominal pressure (Valsalva) throughout wks 0-8; no jogging until wk 6+ (pain-free); hip index (flexion/abduction/adduction/extension) <20% before return to jogging
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-1 immediate), Phase II (Wks 2-3 intermediate), Phase III (Wks 4-5 late post-op), Phase IV (Wks 6-8 transitional), Phase V (Wks 9-12 early RTS), Phase VI (Month 3+ unrestricted RTS)
Notes: Post-operative; avoid Valsalva throughout; HAGOS questionnaire >90% and hip index >=90% for RTS; adductor/abductor ratio >80% using HHD

WB Status: PWB 20lbs step-to pattern wks 0-3; gradually increase to WBAT wks 4-6; wean from crutches by 6-8 weeks
Key Restrictions: Hip flex 0-90 deg, ext to neutral, ABD 0-30 deg, ER/IR 0-30 deg wks 0-3; no active SLR wks 0-8; no active hip flexion days 0-21; no Grade III-IV hip mobilization wks 0-6; no long axis distraction wks 0-8 (labral repair) or wks 0-12 (labral reconstruction); no sitting >30 min first 2 wks; no extreme combined ROM (flexion/IR or flexion/ER); no plyometrics or running until month 4+
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-3 immediate), Phase II (Wks 4-6 intermediate), Phase III (Wks 7-12 late), Phase IV (Wks 12+ transitional), Phase V (Month 4+ early RTS)
Notes: Capsular closure requires limiting hip ER/extension early; labral repair vs reconstruction affects long axis distraction restriction (8 wks vs 12 wks); jogging initiated at 16-18 weeks per surgeon; outcome measure: Hip Outcome Score (HOS)

WB Status: TDWB with crutches wks 0-2; PWB 50% with crutches wks 2-6; progressively wean to FWB wks 6-12
Key Restrictions: Hip brace limiting flex to 45 deg wks 0-2; brace hip flex limit 60 deg wks 2-6 (gradually to 90 deg by wk 6); avoid hip flexion with knee extension; no active hamstrings or hip extension exercises wks 0-6; no running until wks 16-20; no sprinting or jump training until wks 20-22+; discontinue brace at 6-8 wks per MD
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-2 immediate), Phase II (Wks 2-6 intermediate), Phase III (Wks 6-12 late), Phase IV (Wks 13-16 transitional), Phase V (Wks 16-20 early RTS), Phase VI (Wks 20-24 unrestricted RTS)
Notes: Hip brace required wks 0-6; outcomes affected by duration before surgery, tendon retraction, and number of tendons repaired; jump training not until wk 22; LSI hamstring/glute/quad >95% for RTP

WB Status: FWB; non-weight bearing on involved upper extremity during exercise
Key Restrictions: Avoid aggravating activities; activity modification throughout; avoid post-exercise pain/swelling; 70% strength contralateral side before Phase III; 90% strength contralateral side for RTS
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-2 acute), Phase II (Wks 2-4 intermediate/sub-acute), Phase III (Wks 4-6+ late/chronic)
Notes: Non-operative; covers both medial (FCR/flexor tendons) and lateral (ECRB/extensor tendons); outcome measures: PRTEE, DASH

WB Status: Non-weight bearing on repaired upper extremity; hinged brace wks 1-6 (D/C at wk 6 if full ROM)
Key Restrictions: No active elbow flexion or forearm supination until wk 4; no lifting until wk 8; no valgus stress to graft; no ER strengthening until wk 6; no throwing until wk 16+; gracilis graft: no progressive hamstring strengthening until wk 6; interval throwing program at wk 16
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-3 immediate), Phase II (Wks 4-6 protection), Phase III (Wks 6-12 strengthening), Phase IV (Wks 12-16 advanced strengthening), Phase V (Wks 16+ early/unrestricted RTS)
Notes: Palmaris longus graft: wrist compression 5-7 days; gracilis graft: knee ROM immediately/bike at wk 3; KJOC throwers assessment for RTS

WB Status: Non-weight bearing on repaired UE; posterior orthosis at 90 deg days 0-5-7; hinged brace progressively unlocking wks 2-6; begin gradual WB at wk 8 (elbow flexed), extended elbow by wk 10
Key Restrictions: No active elbow flexion or forearm supination until wk 4; no lifting until wk 8; submaximal isometrics at wk 10; bilateral plyometrics after wk 16; single arm plyometrics after wks 20-22
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Days 0-1 immediate), Phase II (Wks 2-6 intermediate), Phase III (Wks 7-10 late), Phase IV (Wks 11-15 transitional), Phase V (Months 4-6 early RTS), Phase VI (Months 6+ unrestricted RTS)
Notes: Hinged brace extension progressively unlocked wks 2-6 (90 deg → 45 → 30 → 20 → D/C); outcomes affected by suture materials; KJOC assessment for discharge

WB Status: NWB in splint/cast wks 0-2; partial progressive WB in boot wks 2-4; FWB in boot wks 4-6 (no SPR repair) or wk 6+ (SPR repaired); wean boot at wk 6 (no SPR) or wk 8 (SPR repaired)
Key Restrictions: If SPR repaired: no inversion/eversion until wk 6; if SPR repaired: no isometric eversion until wk 6; no running until wks 12-16+; LE functional tests >=90% for RTS
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-2 immediate), Phase II (Wks 2-4 intermediate), Phase III (Wks 4-8 late), Phase IV (Wks 9-12 transitional), Phase V (Wks 12-16 advanced), Phase VI (Months 6+ RTS)
Notes: SPR repair status dictates inversion/eversion and eversion strengthening timeline; outcomes affected by tendoscopic vs open approach; PRRS for RTS

WB Status: NWB in splint/Achilles boot wks 0-3; PWB with 3 wedges starting wk 4 (25% BW/week progression); wean one wedge/week (wks 5, 6, 7); FWB boot no wedges wk 8; sneaker with 1cm heel lift wks 9-10; wean heel lift wks 11-12
Key Restrictions: Do not dorsiflex past 0 deg until wk 8; no overt calf stretching until 6 months post-op; no IASTM directly on tendon until wk 16; no running until months 6+; LE functional tests >=90% for RTS
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-3 immediate), Phase II (Wks 4-6 intermediate), Phase III (Wks 7-8 late), Phase IV (Wks 9-10 transitional), Phase V (Wks 11-12 transitional), Phase VI (Months 3-6 advanced), Phase VII (Months 6+ RTS)
Notes: More conservative for re-rupture, revision, tendon augmentation, chronic tendinosis, or comorbidities; Shoe Leveler for uninvolved side throughout boot phase; outcome measures: ATRS, PRRS

WB Status: WBAT from day 1 with brace/tape; immobilization recommended for 10 days for severe sprain
Key Restrictions: Brace or protective tape during WB activities; immobilization up to 10 days for severe sprain; 25 single leg heel raises before Phase III; 90% Y-balance/hop tests for RTS
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 1-2 protection/optimal loading), Phase II (Wks 3-6 intermediate/sub-acute), Phase III (Wks 7-10 late/chronic), Phase IV (Wks 11-16 RTS/functional activities)
Notes: Non-operative lateral ankle sprain; Ottawa ankle rule to rule out fracture; outcome measures: Cumberland Ankle Instability Tool (CAIT) or FAAM

WB Status: NWB with crutches wks 0-2; WBAT in boot wks 2-8; WBAT in shoe with active ankle brace wks 7-8+; FWB in shoe wks 9+
Key Restrictions: No inversion or plantarflexion ROM wks 0-2; no inversion throughout early phases; ankle mobilizations must avoid stress to CFL and ATFL; 90% Y-balance/hop tests and CAIT/FAAM for RTS
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-2 immediate), Phase II (Wks 2-8 intermediate), Phase III (Wks 7-8 late), Phase IV (Wks 9-12 transitional), Phase V (Months 3-4 early RTS), Phase VI (Months 5-6+ unrestricted RTS)
Notes: More cautious for tendon augmentation, peroneal repair, revision, hyperlaxity, or comorbidities; avoid stress to CFL and ATFL with mobilizations; outcome measures: CAIT or FAAM

WB Status: NWB in splint wks 0-2; standing WB in boot only (NWB walking) wks 3-5; WBAT in boot wk 6; wean boot wks 7-8; FWB in sneaker wk 8+
Key Restrictions: Elevate ankle above heart level majority of day wks 0-2; if tendon transfers: no resistance strengthening or stretching of involved musculature until 3 months post-op; no high-impact activities ever; swelling may persist 6-12 months
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-2 immediate), Phase II (Wks 3-5 intermediate), Phase III (Wks 6-10 late), Phase IV (Wks 11-13 transitional), Phase V (Wks 14-16 transitional), Phase VI (Months 4-6 advanced), Phase VII (Months 6+ low-impact RTS)
Notes: Reserved for older adults with lower activity demands; ROM improvement may vary; no return to high-impact sport ever; outcome measures: Y-Balance, Star Excursion Balance Test, PRRS

WB Status: FWB; sling worn at all times wks 0-3 for GH joint support
Key Restrictions: ROM wks 0-3: 90° flex, 90° abd, 0° ER, 0° ext; avoid elbow hyperextension; ROM wks 3-6: 120° flex/abd, 30° ER; no active arm swing until wk 4; ROM wks 6-12: 60° ER; strength 95% uninvolved for RTS
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-3), Phase II (Wks 3-6), Phase III (Wks 6-8), Phase IV (Wks 8-12), Phase V (Wks 12-24 RTS)
Notes: RTS goal by 24 weeks; strength 95% uninvolved side

WB Status: FWB; sling for comfort wk 0-1
Key Restrictions: Avoid active elevation (anterior deltoid) until wk 6; no resistive biceps until wk 6 if concurrent biceps tenodesis; RTS criteria: MD clearance, full pain-free ROM, 85% strength uninvolved, completion of RTS program
Timeline / Phases: 0-1 wk (sling/comfort, pendulums), 1-2 wks (PROM/isometrics), 2-6 wks (progress ROM/strengthening), 6-12 wks (independent program)
Notes: Also covers arthroscopic AC excision and partial-thickness RCT debridement; 85% strength RTS criterion

WB Status: FWB; sling/swathe wks 0-2 at all times (except bathing); sling required at school/crowds/car/sleep wks 2-6; discontinued wks 6-12
Key Restrictions: ROM wks 0-2: 90° flex, 45° abd, 0° ER, 20° ext; ROM wks 2-6: 90° flex, 90° abd, 30° ER, 20° ext; ROM wks 6-12: 60° ER, full flex/abd/ext; throwing/contact RTS at 9 months per strength and physician assessment
Timeline / Phases: 0-2 wks (immobilization), 2-6 wks (PROM/AAROM within limits), 6-12 wks (progress PROM/AROM, strengthening), 12-24 wks (independent program, sport-specific training), 9 months (throwing/contact RTS)
Notes: 9 months for full throwing and contact sport; physician assessment required for RTS

WB Status: FWB; sling 3-6 weeks per MD; may do bike/walking with sling on
Key Restrictions: Avoid biceps tension for 6 weeks; limit ER to 40° and avoid extension/horizontal abduction past neutral for 4 weeks; avoid heavy load/long lever activation wks 6-8; avoid forearm supination with biceps activation; no throwing/swimming/sports until wks 8-12+; RTS cleared by surgeon and PT
Timeline / Phases: 0-6 wks (protection, sling per MD), 6-8 wks (gradual bicep resistance), 8-12 wks (strengthening, no sport), 12-20 wks (low-velocity sport-prep), 20+ wks (sport-specific/RTS)
Notes: With/without subacromial decompression and/or RTC debridement; avoid forearm supination when activating biceps in early phases; RTS cleared by ortho surgeon and PT

WB Status: FWB; sling at all times wks 0-2; sling at work/school/crowds/car/sleep wks 2-4; sling in crowds/sleep wks 4-6; discontinued wks 6-12
Key Restrictions: ROM wks 0-2: 90° flex, 45° abd, 0° ER, 20° ext, 70° IR; ROM wks 2-4: 120° flex, 90° abd, 30° ER, no IR behind back; ROM wks 4-6: 145° abd, 30° ER, no IR behind back; ROM wks 6-12: full flex/abd/ext, 60° ER; noncontact RTS at 24 wks; throwing/contact at 9 months
Timeline / Phases: 0-2 wks, 2-4 wks, 4-6 wks, 6-12 wks, 12-24 wks (independent program), 24 wks (noncontact/throwing RTS), 9 months (contact RTS)
Notes: No IR behind back until wk 6+; noncontact/throwing RTS at 24 weeks; contact sport at 9 months per strength and physician assessment

WB Status: FWB; immobilizer day and night wks 0-2; immobilizer daytime only wks 2-6; no immobilizer from wk 6
Key Restrictions: No PROM wks 0-3; PROM limits wks 3-6: 90° flex, 45° ER, 20° ext, 45° abd; no resisted IR/adduction until Phase III; avoid anterior capsule stretch; keep strengthening below horizontal plane Phase II; no plank/push-ups until wk 16; limited RTS at 4-5 months
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (0-6 wks, immobilizer), Phase II (6-12 wks, no resisted IR/adduction), Phase III (~12-16 wks, eccentrics/plyometrics), Phase IV (4-5 months, limited RTS), Phase V (full RTS)
Notes: No PROM for first 3 weeks; no resisted IR/adduction in Phase II; cycling/running at 12 weeks; limited return to sports at 4-5 months

WB Status: FWB; UltraSling 4-6 weeks (4 wks: traumatic/acute; 6 wks: atraumatic/ligamentous laxity)
Key Restrictions: Protect posterior capsule — avoid extremes of IR throughout; PROM restricted: 90° FF and ER to 0° by 4 wks postop; isokinetic testing at 6 months (≥80% strength for sports-specific program); RTS: 12 months throwing, 9-12 months power/contact, 8-12 months non-contact
Timeline / Phases: 4-6 wks (discontinue sling, PROM/AAROM/isometrics), 6-8 wks (advance ROM, active ER), 2-3 months (full ROM, isotonics), 4 months (eccentric RC, isokinetics), 5 months (advance), 6 months (isokinetic testing, sports-specific)
Notes: Immobilization duration based on injury type; ≥80% isokinetic strength at 6 months before sport-specific program; 9-12 month RTS for contact/power athletes

WB Status: FWB; hold formal PT and wear sling for 4 weeks; home pendulums/elbow AROM from Day 1
Key Restrictions: Hold formal PT 0-4 weeks; sling 4 weeks; ROM limits wks 4-6: flex 0-150°, ER 30°, abd 90°; no IR resistance until >6 wks; discharge when ROM plateau reached
Timeline / Phases: 0-4 wks (sling, hold PT, home pendulums/AROM only), 4-6 wks (PROM/AAROM within limits, isometrics except IR), >6 wks (Thera-Band, scapular stabilization, add IR)
Notes: Dr. Murray's protocol specifically; home pendulums and elbow/wrist AROM only for first 4 weeks (no formal PT); discharge to independent program when ROM plateau reached

WB Status: FWB; ROM limits 0-6 wks: flex 0-150°, ER 30°, abd 90°
Key Restrictions: ROM limits 0-6 wks: flex 0-150°, ER 30°, abd 90°; no IR resistance until >6 wks; discharge to independent program when ROM plateau reached
Timeline / Phases: 0-6 wks (PROM/AAROM within limits, isometrics except IR, begin Day 1-2), >6 wks (Thera-Band, scapular stabilization, add IR)
Notes: Dr. Wolf's protocol; PT begins Day 1-2 unlike Dr. Murray's 4-week hold; discharge when ROM plateau reached

WB Status: FWB; sling at all times wks 0-3
Key Restrictions: ROM wks 0-3: 90° flex, 90° abd, 0° ER, 0° ext; avoid elbow hyperextension; ROM wks 3-6: 120° flex/abd, 30° ER; no active arm swing until wk 4; ROM wks 6-12: 60° ER; strength 95% uninvolved for RTS by 24 weeks
Timeline / Phases: 0-3 wks, 3-6 wks, 6-8 wks, 8-12 wks (ROM goal: full PROM ER to 60° by wk 12), 12-24 wks (independent program/sport-specific)
Notes: RTS goal by 24 weeks; strength 95% uninvolved side

WB Status: FWB; sling/swathe at all times wks 0-6; sling/swathe except exercises/bathing/sitting wks 6-8; sling for comfort (may discontinue) wks 8-10
Key Restrictions: Maximum protection 0-6 weeks; PROM limits wks 6-8: 90° abd, 20° ext, 70° IR (not behind back); AROM initiated wks 10-12; no overhead strengthening until surgeon clearance (wks 12-20+); no overhead sport or heavy lifting until wks 20-24+; indications: massive irreparable RCT, intact deltoid/subscapularis/nerve function
Timeline / Phases: 0-6 wks (max protection), 6-8 wks (PROM within limits), 8-10 wks (AAROM, sling comfort), 10-12 wks (AROM, isometrics), 12-20+ wks (resisted strength, surgeon clearance), 20-24+ wks (advanced strength, sport-specific)
Notes: For massive irreparable RCT without arthritis; requires intact deltoid and subscapularis; no overhead sport or heavy lifting through advanced phase; surgeon clearance required for resistive and advanced training phases

WB Status: FWB; sling full time wks 0-4 (except bathing/exercises); sling for comfort wks 4-6 or in uncontrolled situations
