Key Restrictions: Sling full time until wk 4; sling for comfort wks 4-6; no shoulder flexion isometrics wks 2-6; no UBE until wk 4; goal full AROM without shoulder hiking by 12 weeks
Timeline / Phases: 0-2 wks (sling/PROM/AAROM, pendulums), 2-6 wks (AAROM/AROM, isometrics except flexion), 6-12 wks (AROM, strengthening, independent home program)
Notes: Open and arthroscopic small tear; goal full AROM without shoulder hiking by 12 weeks

WB Status: FWB; ROM limits wks 0-3: ER to 45°, flexion to 90°
Key Restrictions: ROM limits wks 0-3: ER 45°, flexion 90°; discharge to independent program when ROM plateau reached
Timeline / Phases: 0-3 wks (PROM/AAROM within limits, pendulums from Day 1), 3-4 wks (AAROM/AROM, isometrics, scapular), 4-6 wks (Thera-Band, postural education)
Notes: Also covers hemiarthroplasty with intact rotator cuff; discharge to independent program when ROM plateau reached

WB Status: FWB; sling full time wks 0-4 (except bathing/exercises); sling for comfort daytime wks 4-6; wear sling at night through wk 6
Key Restrictions: PROM limits wks 0-2: 90° pure abd, 20° ext, 70° IR (not behind back); subscapularis repair: limit ER to 0° (wks 0-2), 30° (wks 2-6), progress 10°/week from wk 6; no ROM limits from wk 6; goal full AROM by 12 weeks
Timeline / Phases: 0-2 wks, 2-6 wks, 6-12 wks (no ROM limits, AAROM/AROM), 12-18 wks (resistive exercises)
Notes: Open and arthroscopic medium tear; subscapularis repair: ER limit 0° → 30° → 10°/week progression from wk 6; goal full AROM by 12 weeks

WB Status: FWB; sling/swathe at all times wks 0-6 (except bathing/sitting from wk 2); sling for comfort wks 6-12 (may discontinue)
Key Restrictions: PROM limits wks 0-6: 90° pure abd, 20° ext, 70° IR (not behind back); avoid subscapularis activation until 4-6 wks; no reaching behind back until wk 8+; patient must elevate without shoulder hiking before progressing; resistive exercises per surgeon wks 12-18
Timeline / Phases: 0-2 wks, 2-6 wks, 6-12 wks (AAROM/AROM, sling for comfort), 12-18 wks (resistive exercises per surgeon)
Notes: Open and arthroscopic large tear; resistive exercises held per surgeon from wks 12-18; no shoulder hiking is criterion for progression

WB Status: WBAT; crutch-assisted until minimal limp; brace 1-2 wks
Key Restrictions: No resisted leg extension machine at any point; if hamstring graft: no isolated hamstring until 3 wks, no open chain resisted hamstring curls until at least 6 wks; ROM 0-90° by 2 wks, 0-115° by 4 wks
Notes: Preoperative PT visit; functional assessment at 14 weeks (ground clock, unilateral squat, lateral shuffle, jump mechanics); jogging at 12-14 weeks with good quad control

WB Status: NWB 3-4 wks; knee immobilizer 4 wks
Key Restrictions: NWB 3-4 wks; knee immobilizer 4 wks; CKC between 20-70° or full extension only; avoid submaximal CKC 8 wks; active hamstring at 6 wks, resistive at 8 wks; no lateral exercises 10 wks; no pivoting/ballistic ≥4 months; no resisted leg extension at any point
Notes: Functional assessments at 14 weeks and 5-6 months postop

WB Status: TDWB 3-5 days, then FWB in extension; knee immobilizer 3 wks
Key Restrictions: Knee immobilizer 3 wks; TDWB 3-5 days; CKC 20-70° or full extension only; avoid submaximal CKC 6 wks; active hamstring at 4 wks, resistive at 6 wks; no lateral exercises 6-8 wks; no pivoting/ballistic ≥4 months; no resisted leg extension at any point
Notes: Watch for lower leg rotation or heel whip with ambulation; functional assessments at 14 weeks and 5-6 months

WB Status: TDWB 4-6 wks; knee immobilizer 4 wks
Key Restrictions: Knee immobilizer 4 wks; TDWB 4-6 wks; CKC 20-70° or full extension only; avoid submaximal CKC 8 wks; active hamstring at 6 wks, resistive at 8 wks; no lateral exercises 10 wks; no pivoting/ballistic ≥4 months; no resisted leg extension at any point
Notes: Cybex/functional assessment at 14 weeks, 6 months, and 1 year postop

WB Status: TDWB 4 wks; PWB 4-6 wks
Key Restrictions: Knee immobilizer 0-1 wk; long hinge brace 0-30° wks 1-2, increase 10°/wk to 90°, continue 0-90° until 6 wks; ROM restricted 0-70° for 4 wks; no resisted hamstring 4 wks; CPM 6 hrs/day x 4 wks (0-70°); no resisted leg extension at any point; no high-impact/cutting/twisting ≥4 months
Notes: CPM 6 hours/day for 4 weeks; MD visits at day 1, 8-10, 1 month, 2 months, 4 months, 6 months, and 1 year postop

WB Status: WBAT; knee immobilizer for ambulation 2 wks; FWB by end of wk 2
Key Restrictions: Knee immobilizer for ambulation 2 wks; regular patellar mobilization (grade I-II if good mobility ≥3 quadrants); RTS: involved limb deficiencies <10% uninvolved, surgeon clearance (usually 6-9 months)
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Day 1 to 6 wks, OKC focus), Phase II (Wks 7-12, CKC/lateral), Phase III (Wks 13-16, plyometrics), Phase IV (Wks 17+ RTS)
Notes: For extensor mechanism realignment with MPFL reconstruction, use Tibial Tubercle Osteotomy protocol; RTS usually 6-9 months per surgeon clearance

Surgery Type: with Medial and Lateral Retinaculum Repair
WB Status: WBAT; brace locked at 0° for 4 wks
Key Restrictions: ROM blocked at 0° for 4 wks; no flexion past 90° for 8 wks; brace 0-40° wks 4-6; brace 0-80° wks 6-8; RTS dependent on functional sports test
Notes: Return to sports dependent on passing a functional sports test

WB Status: TDWB with crutches 4 wks; hinged neoprene brace 8 wks (locked full extension 4 wks, then 0-30° progressing 15°/wk)
Key Restrictions: TDWB 4 wks; hinged brace locked at full extension 4 wks; no resisted knee flexion 8 wks (passive flexion OK); wean brace 8-12 wks
Notes: Light jog possible at 4 months, agilities at 5 months, sport-specific drills at 6 months

WB Status: WBAT; ROM blocked at 0° for 2 wks; brace 0-40° wks 4-6 per MD
Key Restrictions: ROM blocked at 0° for 2 wks; no flexion past 90° for 6-8 wks; brace 0-40° wks 4-6; brace 0-80° wks 6-8; brace unlocked by 8 wks if quad control good; RTS dependent on functional sports test
Notes: Return to sports dependent on passing a functional sports test

WB Status: FWB in extension 4 wks; knee immobilizer 3 wks
Key Restrictions: Knee immobilizer 3 wks; FWB in extension only 4 wks; CKC 20-90° or full extension; avoid submaximal CKC 6 wks; active hamstring at 4 wks, resistive at 6 wks; no lateral exercises 6-8 wks; no pivoting/ballistic ≥4 months; no resisted leg extension at any point
Notes: Cybex/functional assessment at 14 weeks, 6 months, and 1 year postop

WB Status: TDWB with crutches and immobilizer (blocked 0°) 4 wks; WBAT with crutches wks 4-8
Key Restrictions: TDWB with immobilizer blocked at 0° for 4 wks; no flexion past 90° for 4 wks; avoid active knee extension wks 4-6; regular patellar mobilization (superior and inferior only); FWB by wk 8; RTS dependent on functional sports test and surgeon clearance
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Day 1-4 wks), Phase II (Wks 4-8), Phase III (Wks 8-12), Phase IV (Wks 12-16), Phase V (Wks 16-6 months), Phase VI (6+ months RTS)
Notes: Also covers extensor mechanism realignment with MPFL reconstruction; RTS dependent on functional sports assessment and surgeon clearance

WB Status: WBAT; walker/crutches/cane until cleared by MD/PT; goal: no assistive device by 6 wks
Key Restrictions: ROM goals: 0-90° by 2 wks, 0-100° by 6 wks, 0-120° by 12 wks; compression stockings 2 wks; driving cleared by MD usually 4-6 wks; ice 3-4x/day 15-20 min; change knee position ≥1x/hr when awake
Timeline / Phases: 0-2 wks (ROM 0-90°, WBAT), 2-6 wks (ROM 0-100°, CKC, bike), 6-12 wks (ROM 0-120°, single-leg exercises, stairs), 12+ wks (return to activities)
Notes: Pre-operative PT visit for gait training and home exercise instruction; compression stockings 2 weeks; home exercises 3x daily

WB Status: WBAT with brace; crutches to assist; brace worn while sleeping 4 wks
Key Restrictions: Brace while sleeping 4 wks; ROM 0-90° x4 wks; discontinue brace at 4-6 wks; ROM goals: 0-110° by wks 4-6, 0-130° by wks 6-8, full ROM by wks 8-12; return to sport >6 months with MD approval and functional test
Timeline / Phases: Wk 1, Wks 2-4, 4-6 (ROM 0-110°), 6-8 (ROM 0-130°), 8-12 (full ROM), 12+ (sport-specific, sports test at 3/6/9 months)
Notes: Functional assessment/sports test at 3, 6, and 9 months; no fins swimming until >12 weeks; return to sport >6 months if approved by MD and passing sports functional test

WB Status: NWB 4-6 wks (per MD); TDWB in full extension may be allowed per MD; WBAT wks 7-9
Key Restrictions: NWB 4-6 wks; immobilizer locked at 0° until 4 wks; CKC at 6 wks in full extension or 20-70° only (maintained until 4 months); CKC limited to 90° until 20 wks; avoid deep knee flexion CKC 6 months; active hamstring at 6 wks, resistive at 8-10 wks; avoid pivoting/twisting; quad strength 80% uninvolved before plyometrics, 90% for RTS
Timeline / Phases: Wks 1-6 (NWB/TDWB, ROM 0-120° by wk 6), Wks 7-9 (WBAT, normalize gait), Wks 10-15 (strength/endurance, CKC <70°), Wks 16-20 (dynamic SL, plyometrics), Wk 20+ (return to running, RTS at 6 months)
Notes: Deep squatting past parallel avoided until 6 months; RTS transition begins at 6 months if passing functional evaluation (Vail Sport Test, hop testing battery, 90% quad symmetry); return to sport per functional exam and MD evaluation

WB Status: FWB; elbow brace blocked at 45° wks 2-3
Key Restrictions: Brace blocked at 45° (wk 3: 30°) to full flexion wks 2-3; AROM extension only to -45° wks 2-3; passive supination with elbow at 90° only wks 2-3; discharge brace at wk 4 except at-risk situations; no progressive resisted exercise until wk 8; return to sport-specific at 12-14 wks
Timeline / Phases: Wks 2-3 (brace 45°→full flex, PROM, submaximal isometrics), Wk 4 (discharge brace, AAROM), Wk 5 (AROM all planes), Wks 6-8 (combined motions), Wk 8 (progressive resisted exercises), Wks 12-14 (sport-specific, throwing return)
Notes: ROM progression may be adjusted per surgeon assessment; throwing athletes can start return to throwing at 12-14 weeks; authored by Drs. Hoffman, Murray, and Wolf

WB Status: FWB; tip protector splint continuously until 6 wks
Key Restrictions: No PROM and no strength testing at initial evaluation; tip protector splint continuously with pin protected; pin removed 4-6 wks; mallet finger splint from 6 wks (continuous except HEP); PROM DIP begins at 7 wks only if no extensor lag; wean from mallet splint starting 9 wks (reduce 1 hr/day); discontinue splint at night 12 wks; monitor for extensor lag throughout
Timeline / Phases: 3-5 days (tip protector splint, A/PROM MP/PIP), 4-6 wks (pin removal), 6 wks (AROM DIP 6x/day, mallet splint), 7 wks (PROM DIP if no lag), 9 wks (wean splint), 12 wks (discontinue night splint)
Notes: No PROM or strength testing at initial evaluation; continuously monitor for extensor lag throughout course of therapy

WB Status: FWB; short arm thumb spica splint from 2 wks
Key Restrictions: Post-op dressing 0-2 wks; thumb spica splint from 2 wks (except showering); no lifting until 6 wks; therapy starts at 6 wks; avoid strengthening thumb adductor; light strengthening at 8-10 wks; unrestricted use at 12 wks
Timeline / Phases: 0-2 wks (post-op dressing), 2 wks (thumb spica splint), 6 wks (wean splint, therapy, A/PROM, scar massage), 8-10 wks (light strengthening), 12 wks (unrestricted use)
Notes: FCR stays intact; strengthening focus: 1st dorsal interossei, APB and APL (avoid thumb adductor; avoid MP hyperextension); Revised 6/2020

WB Status: FWB; soft wrist splint at night 2-3 wks initially
Key Restrictions: Therapy begins 4-6 wks post injury/surgery; no strengthening (including grip) until 9-12 wks; soft wrist splint at night initially; wrist extension and supination are priority ROM; grip/pinch strengthening at 9-12 wks only if wrist flex/ext at 50-55°; splint discontinued by 12-15 wks
Timeline / Phases: 4-6 wks (cast/fixator removed, A/PROM, no strengthening), 7-8 wks (scar massage, wean splint), 9-12 wks (grip/pinch and wrist strengthening), 12-15 wks (discontinue splint, continue strengthening)
Notes: Covers post-surgical and post-injury management; if PROM not returning for wrist extension/supination, consider dynamic splinting (confirm no bony blocks first); if concurrent carpal tunnel release, avoid repetitive motion until symptoms resolved

WB Status: FWB; thumb spica splint continuously until 7 wks
Key Restrictions: No PROM and no strength testing at initial evaluation; thumb spica (thumb palmar/radial abduction, IF slight hyperextension, wrist 20° extension) continuously 3-5 days through 6 wks; AROM only from 4-6 wks; PROM at 6 wks; wean splint at 6 wks (reduce 1 hr/day); discontinue splint at 7 wks; monitor for extensor lag throughout
Timeline / Phases: 3-5 days (thumb spica splint continuous), 4-6 wks (AROM thumb IP ext, splint continuous except exercises), 6 wks (PROM, wean splint), 7 wks (discontinue splint, dynamic flexion, putty strengthening)
Notes: No PROM or strength testing at initial evaluation; monitor for extensor lag during splint weaning; EPL is the extensor pollicis longus (thumb extensor)

WB Status: FWB; volar orthosis continuously except during exercises
Key Restrictions: Volar orthosis (PIP and DIP at 0°) continuously except exercises; two template orthoses for SAM protocol (Template 1: PIP 30°/DIP 20-25° flex; Template 2: PIP 0°); exercises hourly, 20 reps per template, wrist in 30° flex, hold extension 5 sec; progress PIP flex at 2 wks (30°→40°) and 4 wks (40°→50°) if no lag; discontinue orthosis at 6 wks; light activities at 8 wks; monitor for extension lag throughout
Timeline / Phases: 3-5 days (volar orthosis, hourly SAM exercises), 2 wks (adjust to 40° PIP flex if no lag), 4 wks (adjust to 50° PIP flex if no lag), 6 wks (discontinue orthosis, strengthening), 8 wks (light activities, progress strengthening)
Notes: Early Active Short Arc Motion (SAM) Protocol per Evans and Thompson; if lateral bands repaired, limit DIP flexion to 30-35 degrees; monitor for extension lag throughout all stages

WB Status: FWB; volar wrist orthosis (30° wrist ext) continuously except exercises
Key Restrictions: No PROM and no strength testing at initial evaluation; volar wrist orthosis (30° wrist ext, MCP 0°) continuously except exercises; no composite flexion until 6-10 wks; protected PROM in-therapy only (3-5 days); AROM tenodesis at 3 wks; composite MP/IP flexion at 4 wks; orthosis only as needed 6-10 wks (if no extensor lag); resistance strengthening at 10-12 wks
Timeline / Phases: 3-5 days (orthosis, protected PROM in therapy only), 3 wks (AROM tenodesis), 4 wks (composite MP/IP flex, monitor lag), 6-10 wks (orthosis as needed, composite flex if no lag, light strengthening), 10-12 wks (resistance strengthening)
Notes: No PROM or strength testing at initial evaluation; no composite flexion until 6-10 weeks; IP joint inclusion dependent on repair site relative to juncturae tendinum; monitor for extensor lag throughout

WB Status: FWB; dorsal protective splint at all times including sleep
Key Restrictions: Dorsal splint (wrist 30-40° flex, MP 60° flex, IP 0°) at all times including sleep; rubber band traction for passive flexion; active extension 10x/hr; place and hold at 3-4 wks; protect until 6 wks; discharge splint at 6 wks with wristlet; tendon blocking/gliding at 6 wks; no composite wrist+finger extension at 6 wks; no resistive activities until 12 wks if good active motion; no precautions at 12 wks
Timeline / Phases: Days 2-5 (rubber band traction, active ext), Wks 1-2, Wks 3-4 (place and hold), Wk 4, Wk 6 (discharge splint, tendon blocking), Wks 7-8, Wk 12 (no precautions)
Notes: If digital nerve injury, delay exercise progression to 3-4 wks; FPL variant: wrist 20° flex, MP 15° flex, IP 15° flex

WB Status: FWB; two alternating static orthoses at all times including shower
Key Restrictions: Two orthoses alternating daily (extension splint: wrist 30° ext, MCP/IP 0°; flexion splint: wrist 30° ext, MCP 70° flex, IP 0°); hourly exercises; joint protection principles introduced at wk 4; remove for light ADLs 3-4x/day in 30-min intervals at wks 6-8; discontinue day orthosis at 10-12 wks per surgeon; night orthosis worn for 1 YEAR; maintain neutral or slight radial deviation throughout
Timeline / Phases: Day 5-7 (two alternating orthoses, hourly exercises), Wk 4 (joint protection), Wks 6-8 (remove for light ADLs 30 min intervals), Wks 10-12 (DC day orthosis per surgeon)
Notes: Night orthosis required for 1 full year postop; maintain neutral/slight radial deviation during all exercises

WB Status: FWB; therapy begins at 6 wks postop
Key Restrictions: Thumb spica splint between sessions from 6 wks (remove for shower only); avoid radial/ulnar deviation until 8-10 wks; strengthening at 10 wks if motion is adequate
Timeline / Phases: 6 wks (A/PROM flex/ext, thumb spica between sessions), 8-10 wks (AROM deviation, dart-throwers motion), 10 wks (strengthening if motion adequate)
Notes: Uses Palmaris Longus-Arthrex system; F-659 Revised 6/2020

WB Status: FWB; thumb spica splint wks 0-6
Key Restrictions: Thumb spica cast/splint wks 0-2 (bag for shower); removable thumb spica wks 2-6; no pinching, grabbing, or lifting until wks 6-8; discontinue brace at 6-8 wks; avoid sustained heavy pinch until 16 wks; return to sport at 16 wks
Timeline / Phases: Wks 0-2 (thumb spica, ROM of uninvolved joints), Wks 2-4, Wks 4-6, Wks 6-8 (wean brace, light strengthening), Wks 8-16 (progressive strengthening), 16+ (RTS)
Notes: Thumb UCL repair (ulnar collateral ligament of the thumb/MCP joint); not elbow UCL; return to sport at 16 weeks

WB Status: FWB; long-arm splint 0-10 days; hinged brace 10 days-6 wks
Key Restrictions: Long-arm splint 0-10 days; hinged brace with progressive ROM 10 days-6 wks; no valgus stress 6 wks-3 months; no overhead triceps or heavy bench press; avoid hyperextension; competitive throwing not until 1 year
Timeline / Phases: 0-10 days (splint), 10 days-6 wks (hinged brace, AROM), 6 wks-3 months (no valgus stress), 3-4 months (arc toss 20-30 ft), 5-6 months (arc toss 60 ft), 7-8 months (straight line 25-50% velocity), 9-10 months (75% velocity), 11-12 months (competitive pitching at 1 year)
Notes: Tommy John surgery (elbow UCL); throwing progression begins at 3 months; competitive pitching at 1 year

WB Status: NWB 3-4 wks; FWB in boot by 6 wks; therapy begins 4 wks postop
Key Restrictions: No active or passive dorsiflexion past neutral until 8 wks; remove heel wedges starting 6 wks; discontinue boot 6-8 wks; discontinue night splint at 6 wks; discontinue heel wedge in shoe 12-16 wks; no gastroc/soleus stretching until 12 wks; single leg heel raise only when double leg heel raise is equal (12-16 wks)
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 4-6: NWB→FWB in boot), Phase II (Wks 6-12: boot to shoe with heel lift), Phase III (Wk 12-discharge: wean heel wedge, unilateral heel raise 12-16 wks, jogging 16-20 wks, agility 16-20 wks)
Notes: Jogging ~16-20 wks once single leg heel raise with good mechanics; agility drills 16-20 wks cleared by MD; monitor incision and tendon scar for mobility throughout

WB Status: NWB 3 wks; FWB in walking boot 3-6 wks; Aircast splint for day-to-day 6-12 wks
Key Restrictions: No passive inversion or forceful eversion 6 wks; avoid plantarflexion > resting position 4 wks; 90° immobilizer for 3 wks; immobilizer for sleeping 4 wks then Aircast 4-6 wks; no running, jumping, or ballistic activities 3 months; bike at 8 wks, elliptical at 10 wks; lace-up ankle support for athletics at 3-6 months
Timeline / Phases: 0-3 wks (NWB, posterior splint/90° immobilizer), 3-6 wks (WBAT in walking boot, Aircast day-to-day), 6-12 wks (increased intensity, full ROM cautious inversion/eversion), 3-6 months (RTS with lace-up ankle support)
Notes: For chronic ankle instability; MD appointments at day 8-10, 1 month, 2 months, 4 months, 6 months, and 1 year postop

WB Status: NWB 4 wks (splint); WBAT in walking boot from 4 wks; discontinue boot/crutches at 6 wks
Key Restrictions: Removable splint (foot dorsiflexion/neutral, great toe hyperextension) for 4 wks; NWB 4 wks; toe flexion limited to 0-10° at wks 3-4; no resistive exercises until 8-10 wks; turf toe protector in shoe from 6 wks onward
Timeline / Phases: Wks 3-4 (passive DF/toe ext, place and hold, 0-10° toe flex only), Wk 4 (active toe flex/PF with ankle neutral), Wk 5 (active DF/PF, passive toe flex), Wk 6 (DC boot/crutches, turf toe protector, full PROM), Wks 8+ (closed chain, resistive exercises)
Notes: EHL = Extensor Hallucis Longus (great toe extensor); therapy begins at 3 wks postop; heel insert in boot should be removed to maintain foot in dorsiflexion/neutral; no resistive exercises until 8-10 wks

WB Status: NWB until 4 wks; FWB in pneumatic boot by 6 wks; wean to shoe with ASO brace 6-12 wks
Key Restrictions: Short leg splint (ankle neutral) 10-14 days, then short leg cast to 4 wks; no active or passive ankle ROM phase I; avoid inversion/eversion until 6 wks; no barefoot walking; no plyometrics or change of direction until phase III; discontinue boot 6-8 wks; night splint DC at 6 wks; no return to high-impact until cleared by MD and PT; 90% calf strength required for discharge
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-4: NWB, splint/cast, no ankle ROM), Phase II (Wks 4-6: AROM, progress to FWB in boot), Phase III (Wks 6-12: wean to shoe/ASO brace, CKC, proprioception), Phase IV (Wks 16-24+: plyometrics, jogging 16-20 wks, RTS)
Notes: Also covers peroneal tendon debridement; jogging ~16-20 wks; agility 16-20 wks cleared by MD; 90% calf strength vs uninvolved for discharge

WB Status: NWB until 4 wks; FWB in boot by 6 wks; transition to supportive shoe with ASO brace by 8 wks
Key Restrictions: Short leg splint (ankle neutral) 10-14 days, then short leg cast to 4 wks; no active or passive ankle ROM phase I; avoid active inversion/eversion until cleared; no loaded plantarflexion initially; no barefoot walking; avoid high resistance or eccentric exercises until surgeon clearance; no running or jumping until later stages; discontinue boot 6-8 wks; night splint DC at 6 wks
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-4: NWB, splint/cast), Phase II (Wks 4-6: AROM, PWBFWB in boot), Phase III (Wks 6-12: shoe/ASO brace by wk 8, CKC, proprioception), Phase IV (Wks 12-24+: plyometrics, jogging 16-20 wks, RTS)
Notes: Jogging ~16-20 wks once single leg heel raise with good mechanics; agility 16-20 wks cleared by MD; 90% calf strength vs uninvolved for discharge; monitor posterior tibialis tendon for signs of tendinopathy

WB Status: NWB until 4 wks; FWB in boot by 6 wks; transition to supportive shoe at 8 wks
Key Restrictions: Short leg splint (ankle neutral) 10-14 days, then short leg cast to 4 wks; no active or passive ankle ROM phase I; avoid aggressive dorsiflexion and resisted plantarflexion phase II; no barefoot walking; no plyometrics until phase IV; discontinue boot at 8 wks; night splint DC at 6 wks; no return to high-impact until cleared by MD and PT; 90% calf strength required for discharge
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 0-4: NWB, splint/cast, no ankle ROM), Phase II (Wks 4-6: AROM PF/inv/ev, FWB in boot by wk 6), Phase III (Wks 6-12: shoe at 8 wks, CKC, proprioception), Phase IV (Wks 16-24+: plyometrics, jogging 16-20 wks, RTS)
Notes: Includes Achilles debridement, calcaneal bone spur excision, and tendon reattachment with suture anchors; jogging ~16-20 wks once single leg heel raise with good mechanics; 90% calf strength vs uninvolved for discharge

WB Status: NWB wks 3-4; PWBAT at 6 wks per MD; FWB progression thereafter
Key Restrictions: Removable splint keeps foot in dorsiflexion at all times; foot must NOT drop into plantarflexion when splint removed; no WB wks 3-4; foot movement limited to neutral or less unless specified; no resistive exercises until 8-10 wks; running and plyometrics only at 16-20 wks and only when cleared by MD
Timeline / Phases: Wks 3-4 (passive DF, place and hold, active PF 10-15°), Wk 4 (active DF begins, active PF 20-25°), Wk 5 (full AROM all directions), Wk 6 (PWBAT per MD, A/PROM all), Wk 8 (full AROM, resistive DF starts, CKC), Wks 12-16 (sport/recreation loading), Wks 16-20 (running/plyometrics per MD)
Notes: Protocol based on hand tendon repair early active motion principles; therapy begins 3 wks postop; tibialis anterior isolation at 8 wks; confirm with MD if repair is near aponeurosis or if tendon was shortened

WB Status: WBAT; stationary bike starts day 1 postop with minimal resistance
Key Restrictions: Stationary bike begins day 1 postop (minimal resistance); passive ROM emphasis on IR and circumduction wks 1-6; no running until wks 9-13; no lunges or plyometrics until wks 9-13; sports-specific training and functional testing not until wks 17-25
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (Wks 1-7: bike day 1, passive ROM/IR, prone lying, heel slides, bridges), Phase II (Wks 5-9: mini-squats, outdoor biking, swimming, lateral step-ups, elliptical), Phase III (Wks 9-13: lunges, water plyometrics, running, agility), Phase IV (Wks 17-25: sport-specific drills, sprinting, functional testing)
Notes: Covers labral repair, osteoplasty, and capsular repair; stationary bike day 1 postop is a key distinguishing feature; presented as an exercise-week chart format

WB Status: NWB in brace locked at 60° flexion 4 wks; TDWB wks 4-6; WBAT wks 6+
Key Restrictions: NWB brace locked at 60° flexion for 4 wks; avoid hip flexion coupled with knee extension for 6 wks; no resisted CKC hamstring exercise until 8 wks; no unilateral resistive hamstring activity until 12 wks; no running until 16 wks; full return to activity 4-6 months per MD clearance
Timeline / Phases: 0-4 wks (NWB, brace locked 60°), 4-6 wks (TDWB, wean crutches, transition out of brace), 6-8 wks (WBAT, CKC loading), 8-12 wks (gentle strengthening, no impact), 12-16 wks (dynamic/single-leg strength), 16-24 wks (RTS when cleared by MD)
Notes: Stationary cycling at 8 wks; outdoor cycling and swimming at 12 wks; running at 16 wks; sitting may benefit from donut cushion early postop; full return to activity 4-6 months

WB Status: FWBAT; walker wk 1; cane wk 2 (nonsurgical side); no device by wk 3
Key Restrictions: Minimal precautions due to anterolateral approach; limit toe-out and hip extension behind plane of body; cautious with external rotation; no forced hip ER; goal: independent ambulation without device by 3 wks; preoperative PT visit required
Timeline / Phases: 0-1 wks (walker, FWBAT, basic exercises), 1-2 wks (cane, stationary bike per MD, mini-squats, bridges), 2-4 wks (ambulate without device, step-up/down progression, lunges, single-leg balance)
Notes: Anterolateral approach has minimal hip precautions (compare to posterior approach); preop PT visit for HEP and precaution education; check with PT or MD before any activity unsure of

WB Status: FWBAT; walker wk 1; cane wk 2 (nonsurgical side); no device by wk 3
Key Restrictions: No resisted hip external rotation for 4-6 wks; NO standard hip precautions required — Direct Superior Technique has very low dislocation risk with normal activities; cautious with hip ER exercises wks 1-2; preoperative PT visit required; check with PT or MD before any unsure activity
Timeline / Phases: 0-1 wks (walker, FWBAT, basic HEP), 1-2 wks (cane, stationary bike per MD, mini-squats, bridges), 2-4 wks (ambulate without device, step-up/down progression, lunges, single-leg balance)
Notes: Direct Superior approach = NO standard hip precautions (contrast with posterior approach); only restriction is no resisted hip ER for 4-6 wks; preop PT visit for HEP and education

WB Status: TDWB first few weeks per physician; increase WB 25%/wk starting 4-8 wks; ambulate without device by 8-12 wks
Key Restrictions: TDWB first few weeks; hip precautions 1-12 wks: IR to 0° only, adduction to 0° only, flexion to 90° only; no hip IR during rotation exercises (ER to 30° only); stationary bike must not exceed 90° hip flexion; no adduction past midline
Timeline / Phases: 0-4 wks (TDWB, crutches/walker, basic exercises, hip precautions), 4-8 wks (increase WB 25%/wk per MD, cane, mini-squats, bridges), 8-12 wks (ambulate without device, aquatic program)
Notes: Strict hip precautions maintained 1-12 wks (IR 0°, adduction 0°, flex 90°); golf when off 2 crutches ~3-4 months; chip/putt ~3 months; driving ~4 months; golf cart recommended first 6 months

WB Status: N/A
Key Restrictions: Posterior splint at 90 deg wk 1; elbow ROM brace wks 2-6 (progressing 45-100 to 15-130 deg); no active elbow flexion or supination until wk 6; biceps isotonics not until wk 16
Timeline / Phases: Phase I (wk 1): posterior splint; Phase II (wks 2-6): ROM brace, shoulder/wrist/gripping exercises; Phase III (wks 6-10): discontinue brace wk 8, shoulder/wrist isotonics; Phase IV (wks 10-16): UBE; Phase V (wks 16-26): light biceps isotonics, plyometrics (two-handed wk 16, one-handed wk 20-22); Phase VI (wks 26+): sport-specific training and return to activity
Notes: SLU Sports Medicine and Shoulder Service; 6-phase protocol

WB Status: N/A
Key Restrictions: Elbow arthrofibrosis; progressive ROM as tolerated with no restrictions from week 1
Timeline / Phases: Acute (wks 1-6): elbow ROM emphasis, wrist/grip exercises, UBE, scapulothoracic assessment; Functional (wks 6-12): elbow/forearm strengthening, PNF patterns, endurance training; Maintenance (wks 12+): sport-specific training, phase out supervised rehab
Notes: SLU Sports Medicine and Shoulder Service; 4-month protocol, 2-3 visits/week; diagnosis: elbow arthrofibrosis

WB Status: N/A
Key Restrictions: Progressive ROM as tolerated with no restrictions from week 1
Timeline / Phases: Acute (wks 1-6): elbow ROM emphasis, wrist/grip exercises, UBE, scapulothoracic assessment; Functional (wks 6-12): elbow/forearm strengthening, PNF patterns, endurance training; Maintenance (wks 12+): sport-specific training, phase out supervised rehab
Notes: SLU Sports Medicine and Shoulder Service; 4-month protocol, 2-3 visits/week; diagnosis: elbow arthritis / loose body

WB Status: N/A
Key Restrictions: Lateral epicondylitis; avoid overloading wrist extensors during acute phase (wks 1-6)
Timeline / Phases: Acute (wks 1-6): elbow ROM, avoid wrist extensor overload, scapulothoracic assessment, UBE; Functional (wks 6-12): elbow/forearm strengthening, PNF patterns, endurance training; Maintenance (wks 12+): sport-specific training, phase out supervised rehab
Notes: SLU Sports Medicine and Shoulder Service; 4-month protocol, 2-3 visits/week; diagnosis: lateral epicondylitis

WB Status: N/A
Key Restrictions: Splint at 70 deg wk 0-1; brace 40-80 deg wks 1-4; avoid valgus stress at elbow; no ER strengthening until wk 6-8; interval throwing program not until wk 14-24
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (0-1 wk): splint at 70 deg, gripping/wrist ROM; Phase 2 (1-4 wks): brace 40-80 deg, pain-free isometrics; Phase 3 (4-6 wks): brace 20-90 deg, light isotonics, avoid valgus; Phase 4 (6-8 wks): DC brace, ER strengthening begins; Phase 5 (8-10 wks): eccentrics, PNF, aggressive shoulder; Phase 6 (10-13 wks): plyometrics; Phase 7 (14-24 wks): interval throwing program
Notes: SLU Sports Medicine and Shoulder Service; 7-phase protocol; 4-month duration, 2-3 visits/week; 2022 version

WB Status: N/A
Key Restrictions: Brace fixed at 90 deg wk 0-1; brace 30-110 deg wk 2; 10-125 deg wk 3; unrestricted ROM wk 4; avoid valgus stress; no IR wk 1; expedited return to throwing vs. reconstruction
Timeline / Phases: Phase 1 (wk 0-1): brace at 90 deg, wrist ROM, pain-free isometrics; Phase 2 (wks 2-5): progressive brace opening, PROM/AAROM, UBE, scapular training; (see full document for remaining phases)
Notes: SLU/SSM Health PT Orthopedic Residency; evidence-based protocol; internal brace allows expedited rehab vs. reconstruction

WB Status: 20 lbs PWB for 6 weeks; progress to FWB by week 8; wean crutches weeks 8-10
Key Restrictions: No active abduction, IR, or passive ER/adduction for 6 weeks; CPM 4 hrs/day; 20 lb WB restriction through week 6
Timeline / Phases: Wks 0-4: CPM, bike, PROM (hip flexion to 90 deg), gait training PWB; Wks 4-6: progress hip flexion >90 deg, bridges, adduction isotonics; Wks 6-8: progress to 100% WB by wk 8, ROM progression; Wks 8-10: wean crutches, progressive LE strengthening, proprioception, elliptical; Wks 10-12: progressive LE/core, balance, treadmill side-stepping; Wks 12+: treadmill running, sport-specific agility/plyometrics
Notes: SLU Sports Medicine and Shoulder Service; arthroscopic gluteus medius + labral tear; adapted from bryankellymd.com; 4-month protocol, 2-3 visits/week

Surgery Type: Femoroacetabuloplasty with or without Labral Repair
WB Status: WBAT with crutches; wean off crutches week 3; FWB by ~week 6
Key Restrictions: Avoid combined hip motions/excessive flexion; avoid early iliopsoas activation; no isotonic hip flexion until week 8; WBAT (not toe-touch) with crutches
Timeline / Phases: Wks 0-6: wean crutches week 3, isometrics (no hip flexion wks 1-2), isotonics (no hip flexion wks 3-4), bike; Wks 4-10: CKC activities (squat, step up/down), single-limb balance, no isotonic hip flexion until wk 8; Wks 6-12: progressive CKC/single-leg strengthening, elliptical, advanced balance; Wks 12+: plyometrics, running progression (track/treadmill program), sport-specific drills
Notes: SLU/SSM Health PT Orthopedic Residency; evidence-based with references; discharge uses mHHS, HOS-SSS, iHOT-12 PROs; updated 7/8/21; covers FAI with or without labral repair
